For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: рҹҮәрҹҮё The Land of Endless Political Violence
item.description: On this day in 2011, President Barack Obama released a copy of his long-form birth certificate to silence racist conspiracy theorists, including Donald Trump. вҖңI am really honored, and I am really proud that I was able to do something that nobody else could do,вҖқ Trump responded. Good morning,вҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 2011, President Barack Obama released a copy of his long-form birth certificate to silence racist conspiracy theorists, including Donald Trump. вҖңI am really honored, and I am really proud that I was able to do something that nobody else could do,вҖқ Trump responded.

Good morning, friends. Prem and Andrew here. Wishing you a wonderful Monday morning wherever you are. Today, we need to talk about вҖңincitement,вҖқ political violence, and who is actually desensitized to violence: the American people, or Washington, DC?

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ we review the bizarre DC political and media response to the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner gunman, IsraelвҖҷs violence in Lebanon shatters any illusion of a вҖңceasefire,вҖқ and the Senate Democratic primary races in Maine and Michigan heat up.

вҖңIвҖҷve been coming to DC for almost 16 years, trying to make change. And IвҖҷm hoping this is the last time I have to come.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what Judi Richardson, who lost her daughter to gun violence in 2010, said in front of the US Capitol last week. Richardson was among several family members of gun victims whom Prem followed around Congress as they begged our elected leaders to finally do something about AmericaвҖҷs unique and horrifying epidemic.

They only had a handful of meetings, and the visit drew little attention. There was no momentum to be found on Capitol Hill for gun reform last week.

Days later, across the city, a gunman attempted to breach the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner, with multiple shots being fired by law enforcement as he ran through the security perimeter inside the Washington Hilton. The incident вҖ“ which took place at a glitzy media gala attended by President Donald Trump, his top officials, and titans of industries вҖ“ set off a firestorm of controversy.

Trump and Republicans are using the shooting as fodder to support building the presidentвҖҷs pet project вҖ“ a massive, corporate-funded White House ballroom. Even before Trump addressed the public, the talking points already seemed to be circulating.

вҖңI donвҖҷt want to hear one more fucking criticism of TrumpвҖҷs new ballroom at the White House,вҖқ conservative pundit Meghan McCain tweeted, setting off an avalanche of similar tweets from right-wing influencers and politicians вҖ“ even though, to be clear, the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner is a media event, hosted by an independent organization, and is unlikely to be hosted in the White House ballroom, were it to be completed.

WeвҖҷre also seeing right-wing politicians and media figures laboring to blame the apparent assassination attempt on rhetoric from Democrats. And weвҖҷre seeing concerns from Beltway journalists about how Americans вҖ“ who live in a nation with more guns than people, and the only country in the world where mass shootings are so commonplace вҖ“ have been вҖңdesensitizedвҖқ to вҖңpolitical violence.вҖқ

What planet have these people been living on?

Among the examples of supposedly worrying rhetoric from Democrats: Michigan Senator Elissa Slotkin called Trump an вҖңexistential threat to democracyвҖқ (which is correct), and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz described Trump as вҖңfascist to his coreвҖқ (fact check: also true). The Republican National Committee accused both of incitement, just weeks after Trump called Democrats вҖңterroristsвҖқ (again) and threatened the genocide of 93 million people in Iran.

Montana Senator Tim Sheehy, for his part, accused the New York Times of вҖңamplifying Islamic terrorists who demand violence against people they disagree with.вҖқ This is the same senator who, last month, helped break the arm of Brian McGinnis вҖ“ a veteran who went to Congress to protest the US-Israeli war on Iran.

CNNвҖҷs Dana Bash (who herself has viciously compared pro-Palestine students to Nazis) on Sunday asked Democratic Rep. Jamie Raskin if heвҖҷs concerned about Democrats using rhetoric such as that Trump is вҖңterrible for this country and so on.вҖқ

Of course, thereвҖҷs no evidence that Democrats or the left are responsible for incidents like this. And TrumpвҖҷs past alleged assailants have a mixed background. Thomas Matthew Crooks, who grazed TrumpвҖҷs ear with a bullet in Butler, Pennsylvania, was a registered Republican.

Political violence is, in reality, a bipartisan issue - though studies show it is more of a right-wing than a left-wing problem. Minnesota Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman was murdered last year by a Trump supporter. In 2011, Democratic Rep. Gabby Giffords was shot in the head by a murderous far-right conspiracist. A left-wing activist shot GOP House Majority Leader Steve Scalise in 2017. Right-wing podcaster Charlie Kirk was assassinated at a college event in Utah last year.

Trump, for his part, is AmericaвҖҷs inciter-in-chief. He regularly accuses Democratic politicians of treason and sedition. He has called for Democratic lawmakers to be executed. He helped foment the deadly Jan. 6 riot at the US Capitol. And yet Sunday night, Trump said that вҖңthe hate speech of the Democrats is very dangerous.вҖқ

Posts from the anti-LGBTQ social media account Libs of TikTok have preceded scores of bomb threats nationwide; that far-right account is now baselessly and hypocritically trying to pin the DC dinner attack on progressive Rep. Ilhan OmarвҖҷs statements and left-wing actor Mark RuffaloвҖҷs statements about Trump.

Finally, some attendees of the dinner have been disturbed by the lack of public response to the incident вҖ“ with one reporter pontificating about how it shows how вҖңdesensitizedвҖқ Americans are to political violence, even in the nationвҖҷs capital.

How can you express such a complaint after attending a dinner warmly hosting a man whoвҖҷs brought so much violence to the world вҖ“ whose administration turns airstrikes into memes вҖ“ and whose allies cheer it on or laugh or look away?

Frankly, if you unhesitatingly attended the dinner just days after Israel вҖ“ which Trump stands side-by-side with вҖ“ killed our press colleague Amal Khalil in Lebanon, you are further desensitizing Americans to violence.

But what response does the Washington elite expect? After mass killing and mass killing вҖ“ here and abroad вҖ“ people have been made to feel powerless. No matter how many thousands of kids demand gun reform, no matter how many tens of thousands protest against the bombing of children, the response from DC politicians is effectively what Trump told Iowans after a school shooting in 2024: вҖңWe have to get over it.вҖқ

While political and media elites are dropping everything to discuss violence now that they got a whiff of it, the violence theyвҖҷve tolerated and ignored has brought us to this moment.

Just last week, there was a mass shooting in Republican House Speaker Mike JohnsonвҖҷs hometown of Shreveport, Louisiana. Afterward, Gabby Giffords sought to meet with Johnson to discuss gun reform. He refused to meet with her, days before a gunman attempted to breach the swanky dinner he was attending.

Just hours after the dinner, five people were shot near Indiana University. That incident, naturally, did not drive nearly the level of news coverage of what weвҖҷve seen since the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner.

Ultimately, even amid the chaos at the gala event, some attendees seemed quite desensitized as well. As the Times reported, Goldman SachsвҖҷs senior chairman Lloyd Blankfein asked a tablemate, вҖңAre you going to finish that salad?вҖқ Another attendee, a top talent agent, went viral for continuing to eat his salad, too. One attendee was filmed grabbing bottles of wine on the way out.

The White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner ended early, but the after-parties continued. Why wouldnвҖҷt they?

Death tolls:

Iran: Iranian state media on Sunday reported, citing officials, that US-Israeli attacks killed 3,468 people.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,509 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 35 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed, according to officials.

And in more war newsвҖҰ

New Iran proposal: Tehran has proposed a plan to open the Strait of Hormuz, end the US blockade, and end the war, while punting talks over its nuclear program to a later date, Axios reported. Such a plan is unlikely to be supported by Trump.

Iran-Russia talks: Iranian Foreign Affairs Minister Abbas Aragchi is in Russia for diplomatic talks on a range of issues, including the war. He is scheduled to meet with Vladimir Putin

Lebanon strikes: The Israeli military issued so-called evacuation orders for seven towns in southern Lebanon on Sunday and launched a barrage of missiles, killing more than a dozen people and continuing to flatten entire villages, despite the ceasefire. The Israeli military said one soldier was killed in Hezbollah attacks on its forces in southern Lebanon.

Solar panels targeted: Israeli forces destroyed solar panels that powered the water station and electricity to the Lebanese Christian town of Debl, and attacked homes, roads, and olive trees. It is the same town where an Israeli soldier broke a statue of Jesus just days earlier.

Trump attacks press: Trump lashed out at CBSвҖҷs Norah OвҖҷDonnell in a вҖҳ60 MinutesвҖҷ interview Sunday after she asked for his reaction to an alleged manifesto from the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner shooting suspect, which read: вҖңIвҖҷm no longer willing to permit a pedophile, rapist, and traitor to coat my hands with his crimes.вҖқ Trump called the interviewer вҖңa disgrace,вҖқ and said, вҖңIвҖҷm not a rapist.вҖқ

Trump v. IRS: The judge overseeing TrumpвҖҷs ridiculous $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS for the leaking of his tax returns questioned whether the president can sue the US government that he himself oversees. The judge set a hearing for May 27.

What First Amendment? The New York Times reported that ICE could deny someone a green card for participating in campus protests or for a social media post that says вҖңStop Israeli Terror in Palestine.вҖқ

Michigan primary heats up: At the first debate on Friday, Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed attacked his opponent, state Senator Mallory McMorrow, over Drop SiteвҖҷs reporting last year that she drafted an AIPAC position paper in what appeared to be an attempt to win over the Israel lobby.

General election starts in Maine? With just over one month left in MaineвҖҷs Democratic primary for Senate, Susan CollinsвҖҷs super PAC launched $2 million worth of attack ads against Graham Platner, the veteran and oysterman whoвҖҷs leading Governor Janet Mills in the polls.

Trusting TrumpвҖҷs DOJ: Republican Senator Thom Tillis said heвҖҷs taking TrumpвҖҷs Justice Department вҖңat its wordвҖқ that its politicized probe into Jerome Powell is closed, even though Trump said, вҖңitвҖҷs not dropped.вҖқ Tillis will now vote to confirm Kevin Warsh as Fed chair, paving the way for his confirmation.

When was the first White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Association Dinner?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Palestinians in central GazaвҖҷs Deir Al-Balah and the occupied West Bank voted in the first municipal elections since IsraelвҖҷs genocide began, and loyalists of President Mahmoud Abbas won most races (Hamas did not nominate candidates in Deir Al-Balah and boycotted the election in the West Bank). Al JazeeraвҖҷs Hind Khoudary reported that voter turnout in Gaza was low, as people were вҖңbusy with surviving.вҖқ

рҹҮІрҹҮұ Mali minister dead: A Mali government spokesperson confirmed on Sunday that insurgents had killed Defense Minister Sadio Camara one day earlier at his house. West AfricaвҖҷs al-Qaeda affiliate claimed responsibility for the attack.

рҹҮёрҹҮҫ Assad-era officials on trial: Crowds gathered outside a Damascus courthouse on Sunday for the first public trial of Assad-era officials. Bashar Al-AssadвҖҷs cousin, Atef Najib, who appeared in court for вҖңcrimes against the Syrian people,вҖқ was the head of political security in the southern Deraa province when teenagers who drew anti-government graffiti were arrested and tortured, a case that catalyzed mass protests.

рҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮ·рҹҮә Russia-Ukraine latest: Russian attacks on the central Ukrainian city of Dnipro killed five people and injured 46 others, authorities said Saturday. In Russia, local officials said a Ukrainian drone attack killed two people in the area of Belgorod, a border city.

рҹҮЁрҹҮҰ Altman apologizes: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman apologized to the people of Tumbler Ridge, a town in British Columbia, Canada, after admitting that the OpenAI account of a mass shooter who killed eight people had been flagged, but the company opted not to alert law enforcement. BC Premier David Ebby called the apology вҖңnecessary, and yet grossly insufficient.вҖқ


RFK Jr. rushed out of the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner on Saturday amid an active shooter situation, with his security team around him, but left his wife scrambling on her own behind him. Not a good look, BobbyвҖҰ

Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said heвҖҷs вҖңnot ruling anything outвҖқ when CNNвҖҷs Dana Bash asked whether he will vacate the seditious conspiracy convictions secured against members of the Proud Boys and Oath Keepers stemming from the Jan. 6 Capitol riot. He also said Trump was within his rights and вҖңhis dutyвҖқ in pardoning the Jan. 6 insurrectionists.

Meanwhile, on CBSвҖҷs вҖҳFace the Nation,вҖҷ Blanche said of gun reform: вҖңI donвҖҷt think thatвҖҷs something that we should be focused on right now in any way, shape, or form.вҖқ

Speaking about the recent Democratic redistricting measure that passed in Virginia, Rep. Ro Khanna said on NBCвҖҷs вҖҳMeet the PressвҖҷ: вҖңIn a game that the Republicans started, theyвҖҷre actually losing. TheyвҖҷre down a seat. And so the Democrats still would welcome an effort to ban gerrymandering.вҖқ

On FoxвҖҷs вҖҳSunday Morning Futures,вҖҷ GOP Senator Ron Johnson said itвҖҷs a good time to вҖңnuke the filibuster for good,вҖқ to pass a funding bill for the Homeland Security Department, while maintaining that it would be a вҖңlegitimate point of viewвҖқ to keep the filibuster if Republicans had been in the minority.

Monday, April 27: BritainвҖҷs King Charles is set to arrive in the US on a state visit. Plus, in a landmark case, the Supreme Court is scheduled to weigh the legality of geotracking warrants to find criminals. Separately, a US district judge is set to hear Elon MuskвҖҷs lawsuit over OpenAIвҖҷs for-profit conversion.

Tuesday, April 28: A London Appeals Court is set to hear the British governmentвҖҷs case for upholding its designation of Palestine Action as a terrorist organization after the High Court ruled the proscription was unlawful. Plus, King Charles is set to address a joint meeting of Congress. Meanwhile, US Federal Reserve officials are scheduled to meet for two days to discuss their response to the ongoing oil crisis.

Wednesday, April 29: The Supreme Court is set to weigh TrumpвҖҷs bid to terminate legal protections for Syrian and Haitian migrants.

Friday, May 1: Marches are scheduled around the world to mark International WorkersвҖҷ Day, also known as May Day.

ZeteoвҖҷs Minnah Arshad, Melanie Riehl, and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

item.title: рҹҮәрҹҮё The Land of Endless Political Violence
item.description: On this day in 2011, President Barack Obama released a copy of his long-form birth certificate to silence racist conspiracy theorists, including Donald Trump. вҖңI am really honored, and I am really proud that I was able to do something that nobody else could do,вҖқ Trump responded. Good morning,вҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 2011, President Barack Obama released a copy of his long-form birth certificate to silence racist conspiracy theorists, including Donald Trump. вҖңI am really honored, and I am really proud that I was able to do something that nobody else could do,вҖқ Trump responded.

Good morning, friends. Prem and Andrew here. Wishing you a wonderful Monday morning wherever you are. Today, we need to talk about вҖңincitement,вҖқ political violence, and who is actually desensitized to violence: the American people, or Washington, DC?

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ we review the bizarre DC political and media response to the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner gunman, IsraelвҖҷs violence in Lebanon shatters any illusion of a вҖңceasefire,вҖқ and the Senate Democratic primary races in Maine and Michigan heat up.

вҖңIвҖҷve been coming to DC for almost 16 years, trying to make change. And IвҖҷm hoping this is the last time I have to come.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what Judi Richardson, who lost her daughter to gun violence in 2010, said in front of the US Capitol last week. Richardson was among several family members of gun victims whom Prem followed around Congress as they begged our elected leaders to finally do something about AmericaвҖҷs unique and horrifying epidemic.

They only had a handful of meetings, and the visit drew little attention. There was no momentum to be found on Capitol Hill for gun reform last week.

Days later, across the city, a gunman attempted to breach the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner, with multiple shots being fired by law enforcement as he ran through the security perimeter inside the Washington Hilton. The incident вҖ“ which took place at a glitzy media gala attended by President Donald Trump, his top officials, and titans of industries вҖ“ set off a firestorm of controversy.

Trump and Republicans are using the shooting as fodder to support building the presidentвҖҷs pet project вҖ“ a massive, corporate-funded White House ballroom. Even before Trump addressed the public, the talking points already seemed to be circulating.

вҖңI donвҖҷt want to hear one more fucking criticism of TrumpвҖҷs new ballroom at the White House,вҖқ conservative pundit Meghan McCain tweeted, setting off an avalanche of similar tweets from right-wing influencers and politicians вҖ“ even though, to be clear, the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner is a media event, hosted by an independent organization, and is unlikely to be hosted in the White House ballroom, were it to be completed.

WeвҖҷre also seeing right-wing politicians and media figures laboring to blame the apparent assassination attempt on rhetoric from Democrats. And weвҖҷre seeing concerns from Beltway journalists about how Americans вҖ“ who live in a nation with more guns than people, and the only country in the world where mass shootings are so commonplace вҖ“ have been вҖңdesensitizedвҖқ to вҖңpolitical violence.вҖқ

What planet have these people been living on?

Among the examples of supposedly worrying rhetoric from Democrats: Michigan Senator Elissa Slotkin called Trump an вҖңexistential threat to democracyвҖқ (which is correct), and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz described Trump as вҖңfascist to his coreвҖқ (fact check: also true). The Republican National Committee accused both of incitement, just weeks after Trump called Democrats вҖңterroristsвҖқ (again) and threatened the genocide of 93 million people in Iran.

Montana Senator Tim Sheehy, for his part, accused the New York Times of вҖңamplifying Islamic terrorists who demand violence against people they disagree with.вҖқ This is the same senator who, last month, helped break the arm of Brian McGinnis вҖ“ a veteran who went to Congress to protest the US-Israeli war on Iran.

CNNвҖҷs Dana Bash (who herself has viciously compared pro-Palestine students to Nazis) on Sunday asked Democratic Rep. Jamie Raskin if heвҖҷs concerned about Democrats using rhetoric such as that Trump is вҖңterrible for this country and so on.вҖқ

A left-wing activist shot GOP House Majority Leader Steve Scalise in 2017. Right-wing podcaster Charlie Kirk was assassinated at a college event in Utah last year.

Posts from the anti-LGBTQ social media account Libs of TikTok have preceded scores of bomb threats nationwide; that far-right account is now baselessly and hypocritically trying to pin the DC dinner attack on progressive Rep. Ilhan OmarвҖҷs statements and left-wing actor Mark RuffaloвҖҷs statements about Trump.

Finally, some attendees of the dinner have been disturbed by the lack of public response to the incident вҖ“ with one reporter pontificating about how it shows how вҖңdesensitizedвҖқ Americans are to political violence, even in the nationвҖҷs capital.

Frankly, if you unhesitatingly attended the dinner just days after Israel вҖ“ which Trump stands side-by-side with вҖ“ killed our press colleague Amal Khalil in Lebanon, you are further desensitizing Americans to violence.

Al JazeeraвҖҷs Hind Khoudary reported that voter turnout in Gaza was low, as people were вҖңbusy with surviving.вҖқ

рҹҮІрҹҮұ Mali minister dead: A Mali government spokesperson confirmed on Sunday that insurgents had killed Defense Minister Sadio Camara one day earlier at his house. West AfricaвҖҷs al-Qaeda affiliate claimed responsibility for the attack.

item.title: вҖҳShockingly Un-ChristianвҖҷ: Catholic Priest on War in Iran, Attacks Against the Pope
item.description: ItвҖҷs no secret that Donald Trump has a history of disrespecting Christian beliefs , but his ongoing feud with Pope Leo XIV over his administrationвҖҷs use of Christianity to justify the war in Iran has taken the attacks to the next level. In an exclusive Zeteo Town Hall with Mehdi recorded last week,вҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
ItвҖҷs no secret that Donald Trump has a history of disrespecting Christian beliefs, but his ongoing feud with Pope Leo XIV over his administrationвҖҷs use of Christianity to justify the war in Iran has taken the attacks to the next level.

In an exclusive Zeteo Town Hall with Mehdi recorded last week, Fr. James Martin, a senior Jesuit priest and editor-at-large of America Magazine, explains why the Trump administrationвҖҷs attacks against the Pope arenвҖҷt just wrong вҖ“ theyвҖҷre вҖңshockingly un-Christian.вҖқ

On Defense Secretary Pete HegsethвҖҷs March prayer for violence against those вҖңwho deserve no mercy,вҖқ Martin tells Mehdi: вҖңItвҖҷs a complete perversion of the gospel.вҖқ He adds, вҖңTo say that people donвҖҷt deserve mercy is about as un-Christian as you could get.вҖқ

Martin also believes that the Trump administration and the Republican PartyвҖҷs brand of Christian nationalism is being weaponized to create an вҖңus versus themвҖқ narrative to justify violence and determine вҖңwhoвҖҷs AmericanвҖқ and whoвҖҷs not. It represents вҖңthe opposite of JesusвҖҷs message,вҖқ he says.

Paid subscribers got to ask Fr. Martin their own questions, including whether Catholic voters are turning against Trump and the limitations of military force under the Catholic вҖңjust warвҖқ doctrine.

Paid subscribers can watch the full video above. Free subscribers can watch a 4-minute preview. Consider becoming a paid subscriber to unlock content like this from Zeteo вҖ“ and also to join future Zeteo live town halls with big-name guests and newsmakers.

***Editors note: This interview was recorded before the shooting at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Association Dinner in Washington DC on Saturday.

Share

item.title: BREAKING: Trump Evacuated From WH Correspondents Dinner After Shots Fired
item.description: US President Donald Trump left the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner after shots appeared to ring out on Saturday evening, and is set to brief the country shortly. Secret Service reportedly apprehended a gunman at the major Washington, DC, function, according to a social media post from theвҖҰ
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
US President Donald Trump left the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner after shots appeared to ring out on Saturday evening, and is set to brief the country shortly.

Secret Service reportedly apprehended a gunman at the major Washington, DC, function, according to a social media post from the president and the Associated Press.

Multiple shots can be heard in video of the dinner, before President Donald Trump was set to speak, and the president, the First Lady, the vice president, and other dignitaries were escorted off the stage. The dinner is the first Trump has attended as a sitting president.

Moments after the loud bangs sent the gala into a state of chaos, both media and political sources at the event told Zeteo that at first there was complete confusion about what had happened. For a moment, multiple tables were convinced it was a false alarm. Minutes, if not seconds, later, conflicting reports and accounts quickly emerged.

Multiple shots rang out before security agents surrounded Trump, according to live video of the scene from Fox 29 in Philadelphia. Police in riot gear with rifles appeared, as agents escorted out the president and the others on stage, as attendees and Trump officials ducked down.

вҖңQuite an evening in D.C. Secret Service and Law Enforcement did a fantastic job. They acted quickly and bravely,вҖқ Trump posted afterward. вҖңThe shooter has been apprehended, and I have recommended that we вҖҳLET THE SHOW GO ONвҖҷ but, will entirely be guided by Law Enforcement. They will make a decision shortly. Regardless of that decision, the evening will be much different than planned, and weвҖҷll just, plain, have to do it again.вҖқ

Twenty minutes later, Trump wrote that law enforcement requested he leave the event. вҖңI will be giving a press conference in 30 minutes from the White House Press Briefing Room,вҖқ he said, confirming The First Lady, the Vice President, and his Cabinet members are safe.

вҖңI have spoken with all the representatives in charge of the event, and we will be rescheduling within 30 days,вҖқ he added.

This is a developing story. Read it in your web browser, where weвҖҷll update you as we confirm more details.

item.title: This Week in Palestine: Israeli Settlers Attack School, Beat Elderly Palestinian Woman, and Escalate Ethnic CleansвҖҰ
item.description: Killing with impunity. Settler violence is out of control in the occupied West Bank, yet itвҖҷs extremely rare that settlers are held accountable. In fact, itвҖҷs often Palestinians who are detained during attacks, even though they were the ones terrorized by Israeli settlers. Just this week, settlersвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Killing with impunity. Settler violence is out of control in the occupied West Bank, yet itвҖҷs extremely rare that settlers are held accountable. In fact, itвҖҷs often Palestinians who are detained during attacks, even though they were the ones terrorized by Israeli settlers.

Just this week, settlers beat an elderly woman, killed three Palestinians in separate attacks, including one on a school, and re-established an illegal settlement that was dismantled more than 20 years ago. And thatвҖҷs just a small glimpse of Israeli violence against Palestinians this week. We documented more below, and we bring you the latest out of Gaza, where Israeli вҖңceasefireвҖқ violations continue on a daily basis.

A quick note before we jump into this week: For now, weвҖҷre sending вҖҳThis Week in PalestineвҖҷ to all Zeteo subscribers, but that wonвҖҷt always be the case. To ensure you get the newsletter every week, go to вҖҳmanage subscriptionвҖҷ in your Substack account, scroll down, and toggle on the button for вҖҳThis Week in PalestineвҖҷ. Please make sure youвҖҷre a paid Zeteo subscriber so you can read past the paywall.

HereвҖҷs вҖҳThis Week in PalestineвҖҷ:

Israeli settlers raided multiple villages, damaging Palestinian homes and beating residents, including an elderly woman in a village outside Hebron, according to Palestinian media.

As the woman, Umm Hussein Jabarin, was taken to a health facility for treatment, Israeli forces reportedly detained three of her sons.

Israeli ministers, including ultranationalist Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich and Defense Minister Israel Katz, celebrated the re-establishment of an Israeli settlement that was dismantled more than 20 years ago, Middle East Eye (MEE) reported.

вҖңWe are abolishing the disgrace of expulsion, killing the idea of вҖӢвҖӢthe Palestinian state, and returning to the settlement of Sa-Nur,вҖқ Smotrich said (a day later, the neighboring Palestinian village received demolition orders for 15 shops, Reuters reported).

The settlement was one of four West Bank settlements that were dismantled as part of IsraelвҖҷs disengagement from Gaza in 2005. The other three settlements are also slated to be re-established, per MEE.

Israeli soldiers and settlers are using sexual violence as part of a broader effort to drive Palestinians from their homes, according to the West Bank Protection Consortium. Researchers documented 16 cases of conflict-related sexual violence attributed to Israeli settlers and soldiers over the last three years вҖ“ a number the researchers note is likely a vast undercount.

The researchers said that more than 70% of the displaced households surveyed for the report вҖңidentified threats to women and children, particularly sexualised violence, as the decisive reason for leaving.вҖқ

Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian boysвҖҷ school in the occupied West Bank village of Al-Mughayyir, killing 14-year-old student Aws Hamdi Al-Naasan and 32-year-old Jihad Marzouq Abu Naim, who had rushed to the school to help after he learned of the assault.

Witnesses, describing the horrific attack to Zeteo, said Israeli soldiers were present along with settlers. The schoolвҖҷs principal said, вҖңThey intended to harm the students. The fire was direct and heavy.вҖқ

Al-NassanвҖҷs father was killed in an Israeli settler attack in 2019 in the same village.

Israeli settlers reportedly killed another Palestinian during an attack on the village east of Ramallah. Settlers shot 25-year-old Awda Atef Awawdeh in of Deir Dibwan in the back, according to Palestinian media, citing health authorities. He had reportedly recently celebrated the birth of his twins.

The UK closed a Foreign Office unit that documented potential breaches of international law by Israel in Gaza and Lebanon because of budget cuts, The Guardian reported.

The closure, officials told The Guardian, means that the Foreign Office will lose access to the largest database of verified incidents of possible international law violations across Israel, Palestine, and Lebanon. The database reportedly includes some 26,000 verified incidents in the Middle East.

Israel killed at least 20 people, including at least four children, in strikes across Gaza on Thursday and Friday, according to reports.

Three of the children were killed on Thursday when Israeli forces hit the courtyard of a mosque in northern Gaza.

Miss last weekвҖҷs edition? Catch up here. Plus, check out more recent essays and interviews from Zeteo:

item.title: Why Zeteo WonвҖҷt Be at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner With Trump
item.description: If it wasnвҖҷt clear from our вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ newsletter yesterday, Zeteo will not be at todayвҖҷs White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner. We will not join our media colleagues in partying with Donald Trump, an authoritarian who seeks to curtail the First Amendment and criminalize journalism , who putsвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
If it wasnвҖҷt clear from our вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ newsletter yesterday, Zeteo will not be at todayвҖҷs White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner.

We will not join our media colleagues in partying with Donald Trump, an authoritarian who seeks to curtail the First Amendment and criminalize journalism, who puts masked agents on the street to racially profile and kidnap people and kill people, who jails foreign students for their pro-Palestine speech, who seeks to denaturalize US citizens, who has made it US policy to summarily execute people at sea, who is massacring children and threatening the global economy with his disastrous war in Iran.

There is no reason to don a tuxedo and celebrate this racist wannabe tyrant. There is nothing to be gained by showing up to the dinner with the hopes of offering any small act of protest. Members of the industry should skip the event instead, and refuse to provide a televised backdrop for TrumpвҖҷs vitriol and hate.

Zeteo is a different kind of news outlet. We are an independent organization funded by subscribers. We do not answer to billionaires or corporate overlords. We seek to expose the truth and hold people in power to account.

ItвҖҷs why the Trump White House regularly attacks us, and our founder Mehdi. ItвҖҷs why the White House has repeatedly refused to grant press credentials to our journalists that would allow our team to ask tough questions of the president and his propagandists.

Zeteo is getting under their skin вҖ“ and we intend to stay there. To do so, we need your help.

If youвҖҷre able, please consider becoming a paid subscriber to

If youвҖҷre already a paid subscriber (thank you!), please consider making a donation to Zeteo.

Every paid subscription and every donation, big or small, sends a message to Donald TrumpвҖҷs authoritarian administration that we wonвҖҷt be bullied or silenced. Your support sends a vital message to the rest of the media, too: thereвҖҷs a better way.

Thank you for standing with us at Zeteo. We simply cannot do this work without you.

item.title: 5 Reasons Why TrumpвҖҷs Blockade in the Strait of Hormuz WonвҖҷt Work
item.description: Donald Trump, a president who increasingly struggles to distinguish between fiction and reality, may well have been inspired by the iconic line from вҖҳPirates of the CaribbeanвҖҷ when he ordered a blockade of Iranian ports two weeks ago: Trump is the worst pirate youвҖҷve ever heard of. But you haveвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Donald Trump, a president who increasingly struggles to distinguish between fiction and reality, may well have been inspired by the iconic line from вҖҳPirates of the CaribbeanвҖҷ when he ordered a blockade of Iranian ports two weeks ago:

Trump is the worst pirate youвҖҷve ever heard of. But you have heard of him.

The spirit of Jack Sparrow lives on in a ship-chasing operation that has been aggressively publicized вҖ“ through countless Truth Social posts, Department of Defense action reels, and braggadocious media feuding from the increasingly politicized CENTCOM public affairs office вҖ“ but is unlikely to achieve its goals. Plus, firing your own Navy secretary right after launching a worldwide naval operation does have an undeniable вҖңmad captainвҖқ flair.

Trump thinks broadcasting the blockade will reassure audiences вҖ“ the skeptical American public, his stalwart MAGA base, and maybe himself вҖ“ that heвҖҷs in control of events, despite mounting evidence to the contrary. Like TrumpвҖҷs other recent escalations, itвҖҷs unclear if Trump genuinely believes he can pressure Tehran, or if he just hopes to create that perception so he feels comfortable making concessions. Either way, the US Navy has to carry out the same mission. Here are five reasons why the blockade wonвҖҷt work вҖ“ and wonвҖҷt last:

Per Trump, the blockade is supposed to cut off IranвҖҷs oil and gas exports, collapsing its economy and forcing the Iranian government to accept TrumpвҖҷs maximalist, if ever-shifting, demands. The first problem is that the US Navy, which is attempting to both block Iranian ports and hunt down Iranian ships around the world, is unable to intercept all of the hundreds of ships in IranвҖҷs вҖңghost fleet,вҖқ which have extensive practice falsifying their location signals and transferring cargo at sea to circumvent sanctions. While the US Navy has boarded several ships and turned back many more, over two dozen Iranian ships may have escaped the blockade in the past 10 days.

Add to that tankers laden with at least 140 million barrels of oil that Tehran sent out to sea before the blockade as вҖңfloating storage,вҖқ which puts about a monthвҖҷs worth of exports outside the reach of the main US blockade, and itвҖҷs not hard to see how Iran was still selling the same amount of oil last week as it was at the beginning of the year, pre-war.

As I wrote last month, when the Trump administration was more vocally considering invading Kharg Island in a scheme to cut off IranвҖҷs oil exports, impoverishing the country wouldnвҖҷt defang key elements of the Iranian war effort. Soldiers whose homeland is under bombardment wonвҖҷt lay down their arms just because they got a pay cut, and even if every factory in Iran shut down tomorrow, IranвҖҷs military still has stockpiles of thousands of missiles and drones it can use to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed. But the factories are unlikely to shut down. IranвҖҷs government has weathered economic warfare from the United States for decades, including the вҖңMaximum PressureвҖқ campaign from TrumpвҖҷs first term, has developed a mostly self-sufficient defense industry out of necessity, and has likely long planned to defend itself on a shoestring budget.

An effective blockade вҖ“ again, still a hypothetical вҖ“ would eventually force Tehran to stop pumping oil, which could irreparably limit IranвҖҷs production capacity. But that could take as long as two months вҖ“ time Trump doesnвҖҷt have вҖ“ and still doesnвҖҷt promise Iran will run out of savings.

Despite rising gas prices, the American consumer is still largely insulated from the multifaceted global economic crisis precipitated by IranвҖҷs blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, now exacerbated by the US blockade. But time is running out. A veteran energy market analyst I spoke to said that within 45-60 days, he expected to see a new American affordability crisis that would be impossible to ignore.

The United States is a net energy exporter and will not experience literal shortages like countries in Asia or Europe that depend on exports out of the now-double-blocked Strait of Hormuz. But as refineries dry up in Asia and Europe, countries there are increasingly turning to US oil and gas, which, as Trump has bragged, is a boon for the US hydrocarbon industry. However, that means US consumers are essentially bidding against Asian and European buyers when they fill up at the pump. JP Morgan has warned that oil prices could still go up another 30%, and gas will blow past $5 per gallon. The global supply shock will collide with elevated US demand in June when Americans start summer vacation travel.

Add to that a fertilizer shortage вҖ“ American farmers do rely on Gulf chemical exports вҖ“ gradually driving up grocery prices, plus a global aluminum shortage вҖ“ yep, also a Gulf export вҖ“ that will touch everything from beer cans to Ford F-150s. While consumer pain may not trouble Trump personally, senior Republicans, including Senate Majority Leader John Thune, have openly voiced concern over Iranflation.

Yes, IranвҖҷs economy is also suffering a crisis that will take years to recover from. But IranвҖҷs authoritarian government isnвҖҷt up for election anytime soon, and by their calculation, austerity and privation are small prices to pay for Iranian sovereignty.

Trump hoped his blockade would force Tehran to the table, or at least allow him to take credit for negotiations the Iranian government was going to join anyway вҖ“ thatвҖҷs probably why the US Navy didnвҖҷt start boarding ships until right before Trump expected negotiations to resume on Tuesday. But Trump was foiled by his own pathological inability to take вҖңyesвҖқ for an answer. Last Friday, IranвҖҷs foreign minister announced Tehran was lifting its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, apparently expecting Trump would reciprocate and the two sides would resume talks. Instead, Trump rejected TehranвҖҷs olive branch and said his blockade would continue, proving to Iranian leaders that there was no point in making concessions, and the IRGC announced the Strait was closed again. The will-they-wonвҖҷt-they negotiations in Islamabad announced for this weekend between Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner, and Iranian foreign minister Abbas Aragchi are missing major decision-makers on both sides, representing a step backward for US-Iranian diplomacy.

A naval blockade is officially an act of war, though Tehran isnвҖҷt treating it as such, choosing to retaliate with ship seizures of its own instead of resuming attacks on US forces. But hostile boardings are inherently risky, and resistance from a shipвҖҷs crew, miscommunications, or accidents could lead to the deaths of US Marines or sailors and renewed escalation. Conversely, if TrumpвҖҷs blockade was somehow effective enough that IranвҖҷs leadership felt existentially threatened, it could carry out threats to pummel Gulf energy infrastructure and have YemenвҖҷs Houthis block Red Sea shipping, putting the final nail in the coffin of Gulf exports and accelerating global supply shocks. Trump would be in an even worse position than he was before the ceasefire: in a shooting war with an enemy that wonвҖҷt give in to military pressure, but now with an economic crisis looming closer over his head, a half-depleted stockpile of ballistic missile defense interceptors, and an adversary thatвҖҷs had weeks to reposition and rearm its forces.

In short, Trump has launched a blockade that hasnвҖҷt blocked Iran from selling oil, to pressure an Iranian government that wonвҖҷt succumb to economic pressure, that supports IranвҖҷs strategy of inflicting pain on US markets and consumers, and that is derailing the very negotiations he hoped to restart. Worst pirate weвҖҷve ever heard of, indeed.

Harrison Mann is a former US Army major and executive officer of the Defense Intelligence AgencyвҖҷs Middle East/Africa Regional Center who resigned in protest of his officeвҖҷs support for IsraelвҖҷs war in Gaza under the Biden administration. He is currently with the group Win Without War and recently became a Zeteo contributor.

В

item.title: рҹҘӮ Journalists: Stop Partying With Donald Trump!
item.description: On this day in 1980, the failure of a military mission to rescue Americans held hostage in Iran resulted in the deaths of eight US servicemembers. President Jimmy Carter took full responsibility вҖ“ and went on to lose the White House to Ronald Reagan in November that year. Hi, everybody. ItвҖҷs Swin .вҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1980, the failure of a military mission to rescue Americans held hostage in Iran resulted in the deaths of eight US servicemembers. President Jimmy Carter took full responsibility вҖ“ and went on to lose the White House to Ronald Reagan in November that year.

Hi, everybody. ItвҖҷs Swin. IвҖҷm in Ohio right now (because I live here, Go Bearcats), so I wonвҖҷt be attending the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner in Washington, DC, this Saturday. I also wonвҖҷt be attending because I have a soul and would like to keep it, and IвҖҷm worried the demonic presence at this yearвҖҷs media cotillion would try to suck it out through my solar plexus, like a scene from an вҖҳEvil DeadвҖҷ movie. But more on that in a momentвҖҰ

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ the Washington press corps gets ready to get cozy with its chief tormentor, horrific details emerge of IsraelвҖҷs killing of a Lebanese reporter, and even a Fox poll finds that voters think Trump is unfit to be president.

If someone you knew kept threatening you and your friends with torture and rape in jail вҖ“ and if that someone kept doing it with a grin вҖ“ would you invite them to a dinner party?

You wouldnвҖҷt. Some lines need to be drawn in the sand.

And yet we are now years into Donald Trump making precisely that threat, over and over, at political journalists who piss him off, and now he has the power of the federal government to help him try to do it. вҖңWeвҖҷre going to go to the media companyвҖҰ and weвҖҷre going to say, вҖҳNational security. Give it up or go to jail,вҖҷвҖқ the president said this month.

Trump has adopted the idea of imprisoning journalists so they get raped as an applause line вҖ“ musing that once a reporter вҖңlearns heвҖҷs going to be married to a certain prisoner whoвҖҷs extremely strong, tough, and mean, he will say, вҖҳYou know, I think IвҖҷm going to give you the information.вҖҷвҖқ

And yet, for some reason, a large number of journalists have dry-cleaned their cocktail attire, so that they may attend a glitzy dinner party starring Mr. Trump in Washington, DC, this weekend.

Is it hysterical to think the president and his Justice Department will soon use the Espionage Act to bring criminal charges against a reporter? It would be weird not to expect the administration to try that, simply because theyвҖҷve privately discussed doing it, and also because TrumpвҖҷs public and blatantly authoritarian threats are happening in tandem with his constantly escalating warfare on free speech. Other administrations in recent decades have come after the press and whistleblowers. But what Trump is doing вҖ“ at the White House, DOJ, his personal lawyersвҖҷ offices, and the Brendan Carr-fronted FCC вҖ“ is markedly more disturbing.

On Wednesday, the New York Times reported that the FBI investigated a journalistвҖҰ because she wrote a story about the bureau providing security and transportation for Director Kash PatelвҖҷs girlfriend. Earlier this year, the Trump administration raided a Washington Post reporterвҖҷs home. Team Trump has been trying to jail Don Lemon and another independent reporter, Georgia Fort, because they did journalism. HeвҖҷs turned the FCC into a personal machine for censorship and score-settling. And heвҖҷs been suing media organizations and shaking them down for eight-figure payments to his вҖңlibraryвҖқ fund. All this would be atrocious on its own; what makes it uniquely abominable is that this is merely a fraction of the Trump-Vance administrationвҖҷs sustained assault on the First Amendment.

And now Trump has accepted an invitation from the press corps and is planning to attend the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner, for the first time as president and as the undoubted star of the evening.

The annual dinner and its stretch of corporate-sponsored nightlife has long been a depraved affair, where political journalists make nice with the people they are supposed to aggressively cover. IвҖҷve indulged too but I can honestly say the only time I came close to enjoying myself was when some of my then Daily Beast colleagues and I drank way too much and started loudly making fun of the speakers. Folks at a nearby table kept shooting us death stares.

The dinner is ostensibly a fundraiser and a celebration of free speech and a free press. For the sake of argument, IвҖҷll take the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Association at its word, that this is at least in part what it stands for. Therefore, speaking as a reporter who has covered Trump since 2015, I will say that I have absolutely no desire to party with a mentally decayed, bumbling, and boring madman who, among numerous other outrages, deeply relishes threatening my industry colleagues with prison rape, just because journalists at times report information he does not want reported.

ItвҖҷs one of the easiest choices IвҖҷve ever made. I encourage others to make the same decision.

Some members of the media elite intend to go to the dinner out of tradition or even to offer an implicit protest (in the form of a pocket square?). But there is literally nothing to be gained. Whatever pleasantries, or finger-wagging, are achieved will be dwarfed by TrumpвҖҷs willingness to use the media as a prop, for his Two Minutes Hate disguised as a supposed comedy routine.

Who knows what garbage Trump will say on Saturday night, but two sources with knowledge of the drafting process tell me his standard barbs at the media вҖ“ including вҖңjokesвҖқ about ratings, lawsuits, and prosecutions вҖ“ were heavily kicked around the White House writersвҖҷ room. They want to get a rise out of the pearl-clutching journalists, then sneer that the media canвҖҷt take a joke, all as the Trump-Vance administration keeps ramping up its blitz against the free press.

Fuck that. IвҖҷd rather waterboard myself than put on a tuxedo and be used as a prop in a fascist game show hostвҖҷs ritual of self-love and smug hostility.

There is a reason President Trump, having refused to attend these shindigs before, seems delighted to head over this time: He seems utterly convinced that enough of his enemies in the media have been beaten into submission, on the losing end of a massive joke and con job.

National political journalists of all stripes and leanings have a lot of hard work to do to prove him and his gang wrong. One small step in the right direction would be declining an opportunity to party with the man who keeps telling you heвҖҷd like to have you beaten and assaulted in jail вҖ“ unless you beg for mercy and sell out your sources.

But to each their own.

Death tolls:

Iran: An Iranian official said this week that the death toll from US-Israeli strikes is at 3,375, including 383 children. It was unclear whether the death toll also included military and security forces.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,294 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to figures from the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 34 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war newsвҖҰ

вҖҳShoot and killвҖҷ: Trump said the US would target and вҖңshoot to killвҖқ boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, as Iranian vessels did so. Trump also claimed the US was clearing the Strait.

Pentagon denial: The Department of Defense denied a Washington Post report that it told Congress clearing mines from the Strait would take six months after any peace deal.

Dire threat: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz issued a horrific warning to Iran, saying Israel was вҖңawaiting a green light from the United States вҖ“ first and foremost to complete the elimination of the Khamenei family and to push Iran back into a dark age.вҖқ

вҖҳCeasefireвҖҷ extended: Trump announced a three-week extension of a Lebanon ceasefire Israel has repeatedly and lethally ignored, writing that the US will вҖңwork with LebanonвҖҰ to help it protect itself from Hezbollah.вҖқ

Watchdog silenced: The UK closed a Foreign Office unit which tracked potential breaches of international law by Israel in Lebanon and Gaza because of budget cuts, The Guardian reported.

Even as Donald Trump was blathering about the ceasefire he thinks Israel is observing in Lebanon, horrifying details about IsraelвҖҷs killing of the reporter Amal Khalil became public.

Khalil, who was 43 and worked for the al-Akhbar newspaper, was killed by a triple-tap strike on Wednesday. As described by her colleague Zaynab Faraj to The National, the two women first saw a strike kill two civilians in a car. Both wearing protective equipment clearly marked вҖңPRESS,вҖқ the pairвҖҷs own car was then hit, setting Khalil on fire. The two women took refuge in a house, from where Khalil managed to call family members, rescuers, and the Lebanese military. But would-be rescuers were fired on by Israelis before a drone destroyed the house. When she was eventually rescued, she was badly wounded.

The ninth journalist killed in Lebanon this year, Khalil was targeted a day after Israel responded to international outcry over the destruction of a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon by jailing soldiers and replacing the statue. It faces no such pressure over its killings of journalists.

Sara Qudah, regional director of the Committee to Protect Journalists, told Al Jazeera: вҖңThe Israeli militaryвҖҷs obstruction of medical crews from rescuing wounded civilians is a brutal and recurring crime we have already witnessed in Gaza and now again in Lebanon.вҖқ

KhalilвҖҷs killing, Qudah said, вҖңmust be a wake-up call for the international community to enforce international law, urgently investigate IsraelвҖҷs 262 killings of journalists across the region, and hold all those responsible to account.вҖқ

Do you think Amal Khalil will get a mention by the press-celebrating folks at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner on Saturday night? Of. Course. Not.

Crazy like a Fox: A poll from Donald TrumpвҖҷs favorite network found that 55% of Americans do not think he has вҖңthe mental soundness to serve effectively as president.вҖқ

ICE on ice: NBC News said ICE agents have been instructed to cease entering homes without a judicial warrant, which has fueled opposition to TrumpвҖҷs immigration policies.

Epstein review: The DOJ internal watchdog said it would review the departmentвҖҷs compliance with the Epstein Files Transparency Act. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche admits millions of files remain secret but claims theyвҖҷre not relevant.

Citizens caned: The DOJ has identified 384 foreign-born Americans whose citizenship it will revoke, the New York Times reported. It wasnвҖҷt clear how the number was reached.

Bets off: A special forces soldier involved in the raid that captured Venezuelan President NicolГЎs Maduro was charged with illegally betting on the operation, making $400,000.

Nudnick speaks: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told Congress just one person had been approved for a Trump вҖңGold CardвҖқ visa. It was swiftly pointed out that last year, Lutnick said вҖң70,000вҖқ had applied for the $5 million immigration perk.

Sorry, Kash: The same week FBI Director Kash Patel filed a defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic, a federal judge dismissed a separate suit against former FBI official and ex-MSNBC contributor Frank Figliuzzi over comments he made about Patel frequenting nightclubs on вҖҳMorning Joe.вҖҷ

Which music star claimed in January to have been given a Trump вҖңGold CardвҖқ visa, only for the White House to tell reporters it was merely a вҖңmementoвҖқ?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

Are Trump and his party, finally, down for the count?

Join ZeteoвҖҷs John Harwood for a Substack Live at 4pm ET (1pm PT, 8pm GMT) today with The BulwarkвҖҷs Sarah Longwell.

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Palestinians in the Gaza city of Deir alвҖ‘Balah are set to head to the polls tomorrow in the first election held in the Strip since Hamas won legislative elections in the enclave in 2006.

рҹҢҚ Global hunger on the rise: Some 266 million people experienced high levels of acute food insecurity in 47 countries last year, a UN-backed report said. The share of the analyzed population in this category has nearly doubled since 2016, with two famines вҖ“В in Gaza and Sudan вҖ“ declared last year, the Global Report on Food Crises noted.

рҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮӯрҹҮәрҹҮӘрҹҮә Ukraine aid: The EU approved a вӮ¬90 billion ($105 billion) loan for Kyiv after Hungary dropped its months-long veto. Russian oil deliveries to Hungary and Slovakia have resumed after a long pause due to damage to a critical pipeline.

рҹҮөрҹҮӯ Crimes Against Humanity: The International Criminal Court is charging former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte with crimes against humanity, saying it found substantial evidence to suggest he is responsible for dozens of murders in his anti-drug crackdown.

рҹҮҝрҹҮҰ Finance violation: South African National Police Commissioner Fannie Masemola was suspended after allegedly violating federal finance laws by granting a 360 million rand ($21.7 million) contract to a company run by a man suspected of links to organized crime.

вҖңWe donвҖҷt have to defend our racists because we donвҖҷt know who they are,вҖқ he says, the same day the president publishes an openly white supremacist post on his social media site, targeting Indian and Chinese Americans.

Graham Platner Went to Hell and Back. He Has a Simple Message for Democrats

Progressive oysterman Graham Platner is garnering immense political momentum in his race to challenge GOP Senator Susan Collins in Maine. What can Democrats learn from his success in the field? They need to become the antiwar party to win back voters. For the New York Times, Ben Rhodes delves into how PlatnerвҖҷs antiwar messaging can serve as a model for Democrats to become serious in taking back Congress.

Inside the Heart and Mind of Hasan Piker

For weeks now, mainstream media and party leaders have been outraged over one of the largest progressive voices in US politics: Hasan Piker. And while they attempt to spin the narrative on who Piker truly is, many truly donвҖҷt know the man on the screen. ZeteoвҖҷs Prem Thakker traveled to Los Angeles and sat down with Piker to get a deeper look at his story and his perspective on the current political landscape.

FBI Director Kash Patel is furious over The Atlantic writer Sarah FitzpatrickвҖҷs article exposing his drinking problem and erratic behavior. But even as Patel pursues a lawsuit against The Atlantic, Fitzpatrick is not backing down from her reporting. She joins host Hanna Rosin to provide the inside scoop on her reporting process behind her hard-hitting piece.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

But even as Patel pursues a lawsuit against The Atlantic, Fitzpatrick is not backing down from her reporting. She joins host Hanna Rosin to provide the inside scoop on her reporting process behind her hard-hitting piece.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

item.title: вҖҳTomorrow Can, in Fact, Be Better Than TodayвҖҷ - Watch Zohran MamdaniвҖҷs Speech at ZeteoвҖҷs Party
item.description: Watch now (2 mins) | The New York mayor joined Mehdi and ZeteoвҖҷs special guests to celebrate our second birthday in New York City on Wednesday evening. ItвҖҷs been two years вҖ“ two years! вҖ“ since Zeteo was launched, and weвҖҷre now a little older, a lot bigger, and plenty of awards heavier. To celebrateвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Watch now (2 mins) | The New York mayor joined Mehdi and ZeteoвҖҷs special guests to celebrate our second birthday in New York City on Wednesday evening.

ItвҖҷs been two years вҖ“ two years! вҖ“ since Zeteo was launched, and weвҖҷre now a little older, a lot bigger, and plenty of awards heavier. To celebrate our second birthday, we went back to the amazing folks at the acclaimed вҖҳArabian-French bistroвҖҷ and MehdiвҖҷs favorite Lebanese spot in New York City, Au ZaвҖҷatar, but this time, with a very special guest: the one and only, Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

вҖңIt is a real pleasureвҖҰ to be here to celebrate the work that all of you have done,вҖқ Mayor Mamdani told ZeteoвҖҷs guests at the party, who included some of the biggest names in politics, Hollywood, academia, and entertainment. The crowd was captivated as the mayor briefly shared why he believes what we at Zeteo do is so important for democracy

New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani giving remarks at ZeteoвҖҷs second anniversary party at Au ZaвҖҷatar in New York City on April 23, 2026. Photo by Haseeb Amin.

вҖңWhat you all have shown in a time where it is such a challenging media landscape, is the ability to still tell the same stories that have so often been overlooked to the very people who have lost faith in politics.вҖқ

ItвҖҷs not every day that the super-popular democratic socialist mayor of New York shows up at your birthday party, and Mamdani did more than just give a speech; he mingled with guests and ended up in a lot of photos!

If you enjoyed this speech, above, which includes MehdiвҖҷs light-hearted introduction for the mayor, keep an eye on your inboxes as we will soon share MehdiвҖҷs full speech that followed Mayor MamdaniвҖҷs вҖ“ not an easy person to follow! вҖ“ and photos of Mehdi, Mamdani, and all of ZeteoвҖҷs big-name guests who gathered to celebrate two years of honest, hard-hitting, and unfiltered journalism.

This event was sponsored by the amazing team at Substack, which has supported and hosted Zeteo from the very beginning. A special thanks to our wonderful friends at Oneworld Publications/Penguin Random House, and Henry Holt & Company for gifting our guests copies of fantastic books from the talented Molly Crabapple and Andrea Freeman, and to Ahmad Tea for the wonderful вҖҳTea BouquetвҖҷ that accompanied the incredible reads.

And the biggest of thanks to YOU ALL, our loyal subscribers, who make our work possible and our coverage meaningful. We can only be as great as the people who support us, and we truly have the greatest of supporters to keep us independent in a media industry that is anything but.

Onwards and upwards!

Thank you for being a paid subscriber to Zeteo, your support helps keep this media movement alive. If youвҖҷd like to wear your support, donвҖҷt forget to check out ZeteoвҖҷs Shop where youвҖҷll find hoodies, tote bags, and more вҖ“ and paid subscribers get a 20% discount.

For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: Should the Left and Right Unite Against Israel? Naomi and Mehdi Debate How To Respond to Tucker, MTG, and the MAGAвҖҰ
item.description: The online MAGA movement is going through another major meltdown вҖ“ as far-right influencers and pundits like Tucker Carlson , Candace Owens , and Marjorie Taylor Greene speak out against Trump and IsraelвҖҷs war on Iran, as well as the administrationвҖҷs cover-up of the Epstein files. But what doesвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
The online MAGA movement is going through another major meltdown вҖ“ as far-right influencers and pundits like Tucker Carlson, Candace Owens, and Marjorie Taylor Greene speak out against Trump and IsraelвҖҷs war on Iran, as well as the administrationвҖҷs cover-up of the Epstein files.

But what does this splintering mean for the left? Should people like MTG be embraced by the left, as Rep. Ilhan Omar has suggested, even as she continues to spread vaccine misinformation and celebrate Jan. 6 rioters? Could this splintering actually lead to a new, successful political coalition? Should it?

In an in-person conversation in Washington DC for the latest episode of вҖҳUnshocked,вҖҷ Naomi and Mehdi cover it all and more, with the two debating what the leftвҖҷs relationship should look like with the new anti-war, anti-Epstein right.

HereвҖҷs a preview of some of the topics they cover:

What right-wing influencersвҖҷ criticism of Israel is actually about (вҖңAre they pro-Palestine or are they just anti-Israel?вҖқ Mehdi asks)

If lefties should be criticized for going on right-wing shows, like Bernie Sanders was when he went on Joe Rogan back in 2020 (вҖңThat was a real low point inвҖҰ cancel culture,вҖқ Naomi says)

The vacuum that centrist politicians have created for these pundits (вҖңWhy canвҖҷt Democrats say what Marjorie Taylor Greene is saying?вҖқ Mehdi asks)

What it would take to build a successful left-right coalition (вҖңMamdani is showing what the playbook could look like,вҖқ Naomi says)

Paid subscribers can watch the entire 37-minute conversation between Mehdi and Naomi. Free subscribers can watch a 8-minute preview. Consider upgrading to a paid subscription today to support the work Zeteo does, and to never hit a paywall again.

If you like this conversation, then be on the lookout for more вҖҳUnshockedвҖҷ episodes from Naomi and Mehdi coming your way soon, as they pull viewers вҖңout of shockвҖқ through analysis, facts, and history.

Check out ZeteoвҖҷs other recent stories:

item.title: BREAKING: Kuwait Acquits Prominent Journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin
item.description: A Kuwaiti court acquitted Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin of all charges on Thursday after he spent nearly two months in prison, according to his familyвҖҷs legal team . The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) said more details are forthcoming, but heвҖҷs expected to be releasedвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
A Kuwaiti court acquitted Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin of all charges on Thursday after he spent nearly two months in prison, according to his familyвҖҷs legal team.

The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) said more details are forthcoming, but heвҖҷs expected to be released imminently.

вҖңWe are relieved that Ahmed Shihab-Eldin has been found innocent after 52 days in detention,вҖқ said CPJ CEO Jodie Ginsberg. вҖңAhmedвҖҷs freedom and safety remain our topmost priority and we will continue to closely monitor his case.вҖқ

Shihab-Eldin, an award-winning journalist of Palestinian descent who has worked with Al Jazeera, the New York Times, PBS, and the BBC, among other outlets, was arrested while visiting family in Kuwait on March 3.

The accusations against him were never made public, but he was believed to have been charged with вҖңspreading false information, harming national security, and misusing his mobile phone,вҖқ according to CPJ, which called such accusations вҖңvague and overly broadвҖқ and often used to вҖңsilence independent journalists.вҖқ

Shihab-Eldin, who last posted online on March 2, had shared publicly available videos and images, including a geolocated video verified by CNN, showing a US fighter jet crash near a US air base in Kuwait. The posts appeared to show a friendly fire incident in which Kuwaiti air defenses shot down three US planes. No pilots or crew members were killed.

His arrest came amid a wider crackdown on speech and the press across the Gulf amid the Iran war.

KuwaitвҖҷs Ministry of Interior warned on March 2 against filming or publishing videos or information related to Iranian attacks. About two weeks later, the country enacted a law against disseminating news, statements, or false rumors with the intent to undermine confidence in the countryвҖҷs вҖңmilitary entities,вҖқ according to CPJ. Anyone convicted of breaking the law faced up to 10 years in prison.

Drop Site, citing Al-Rai newspaper, noted that ThursdayвҖҷs verdicts were the first rulings of a newly established court overseeing alleged state security and terrorism crimes. Of the 137 verdicts handed down on Thursday related to charges of вҖңinciting sectarian strife on social media platforms,вҖқ 18 included sentences of at least three years in prison, per Drop Site.
