item.title: TrumpвҖҷs Omnishambles Deepens With Dire Strait News
item.description: On this day in 1937, eager law student Richard Milhous Nixon applied to be a special agent for the FBI. The application was unsuccessful. Years later, FBI director J Edgar Hoover would call the FBIвҖҷs loss вҖңultimately вҖҰ the countryвҖҷs gainвҖқ вҖ“ a judgment historians of the 37th president might beвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1937, eager law student Richard Milhous Nixon applied to be a special agent for the FBI. The application was unsuccessful. Years later, FBI director J Edgar Hoover would call the FBIвҖҷs loss вҖңultimately вҖҰ the countryвҖҷs gainвҖқ вҖ“ a judgment historians of the 37th president might be reluctant to accept.

Greetings, Martin here, honored to be your вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ author for the very first time. Before the main event, I want to point you to MehdiвҖҷs monologue on Pete HegsethвҖҷs Christian Crusade, aka the secretary of defenseвҖҷs justifications of war against a religious fundamentalist threat to world peace, expressed in the language of a religious fundamentalist threat to world peace. There is a terrifying Christian bigot in the Pentagon, and weвҖҷd all be better off if there werenвҖҷt.

Now to todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ in which the Pentagon gives Congress some very bad news indeed, a racist Supreme Court rant shows even Trump knows his own hand-picked justices are about to hand him his hat, and TrumpвҖҷs Navy secretary and media CEO are out.

On Wednesday, the Washington Post published a short report that highlighted a very big problem for Donald Trump as he tries to end his catastrophic war and arrest plummeting polling.

According to the Post, the Pentagon on Tuesday told Congress that clearing the Strait of Hormuz of Iranian mines could take six months вҖ“ and efforts to do so cannot start until the war is over.

The Strait is a key waterway for trade, particularly in oil, fertilizer, and other commodities. The Post is one of the mainstream outlets Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has tried to stop reporting on the Pentagon. HasnвҖҷt worked. Citing three sources, the paper said the timeline for clearing the Strait was вҖңmet with frustration by Democrats and Republicans alike,вҖқ as вҖңperhaps the clearest sign that gasoline and oil prices could remain elevated long after any peace deal is reached.вҖқ

Impervious to reality as ever, Trump insists Tehran will soon вҖң cry uncle.вҖқ But yesterday, as Iran attacked two cargo vessels in the Strait, a Nephew had ominous words for the White House.

Though mines in the Strait might not cause a вҖңtotal interruptionвҖқ of shipping traffic, said Richard Nephew, an Iran expert at Columbia University, вҖңYouвҖҷre not going to have many people wanting to run that riskвҖқ and send ships into death-strewn waters.

Furthermore, even if peace can be found and a six-month effort to clear the Strait then starts, it seems Iran wonвҖҷt be much help, given it has reportedly indicated it doesnвҖҷt know where all its mines are.

Behind every such headline lingers a dread fact for Trump: the midterm elections are just six months away. And every day, in every way, the administration is lurching further into the feared world of the вҖң omnishambles.вҖқ

Polling shows it. This week, before the bad news about the Strait and the mines, AP/NORC found TrumpвҖҷs approval on the economy at just 30%, down eight points from March, while 72% of Americans said the country was heading in the wrong direction. TrumpвҖҷs favorability rating was also down, by five points to 33%.

Nor is it just the war and the economy that are driving people away from Trump and the Republicans prostrated before him. Over at Reuters/IPSOS, a majority of respondents gave the thumbs down to TrumpвҖҷs hardline immigration policy.

Images of state brutality and murder may not have swamped our screens since January, when ICE ran amok in Minneapolis, but as Sarah вҖӢPierce, director of social policy for Third Way, told Reuters: вҖңPeople were being pulled out of cars, a priest shot with pepper balls, and Americans killed before our eyes. I donвҖҷt think those images are going to go away anytime soon.вҖң

NBC News found TrumpвҖҷs approval rating slumped to 37%, while nearly 70% of respondents disapproved of TrumpвҖҷs handling of inflation and the cost of living.

And amid it all, the Iran war drags on, with Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz.

To use words Trump might understand вҖ“ his own вҖ“ for his administration, this is вҖң Not good, not good at all.вҖң

Death tolls:

Iran: An Iranian official said this week that the death toll from US-Israeli strikes is at 3,375, including 383 children. It was unclear whether the death toll also included military and security forces.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,294 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to figures from the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 34 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war newsвҖҰ

Resolution rejected: The Senate rejected a war powers resolution to rein in TrumpвҖҷs war on Iran вҖ“ for a fifth time вҖ“ in a vote of 46 to 51.

Trump claim: The president said he prevented Iran from executing вҖңeight women protestersвҖқ вҖ“ but provided no further details. Iran said no such executions had been planned.

No relation: Two Iranian women held in the US are not related to the late military commander Qasem Soleimani as claimed by the Trump administration, Drop Site News reported, adding that one of the women is now seriously ill in a Texas ICE facility.

Israel kills another journalist: Journalist Amal Khalil was killed in southern Lebanon by Israeli strikes after getting trapped under rubble and being unable to access emergency services, as IsraelвҖҷs continued attacks blocked rescuers.

It seems Donald Trump knows the Supreme Court is going to rule against him again, over his laughable attempt to end birthright citizenship as enshrined by the 14th Amendment since 1868.

вҖңHow can the Democrats not like how the US Supreme Court votes,вҖқ the president began a raving madmanвҖҷs screed вҖ“ sorry, вҖңpostвҖқ вҖ“ on Wednesday. вҖңThe Democrat Justices stick together like glueвҖҰ [and] ALWAYS vote as a group, or BLOCK, even that new, Low IQ person, that somehow found her way to the bench.вҖқ

That was a vile, racist, and misogynistic reference to Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, the double Harvard grad and esteemed judge appointed by Joe Biden in 2022.

Ranting on, Trump claimed, вҖңthe Republican Justices donвҖҷt stick together, they give the Democrats win after win.вҖқ It was, of course, nonsense, about a court with three Trump picks among six right-wingers who mostly rule in his favor and who helped him return to power, bestowing broad immunity and rejecting attempts to disqualify him on constitutional grounds.

Regardless, Trump called the courtвҖҷs recent rejection of his tariffs вҖңan unnecessary and expensive slap in the face to the USA,вҖқ adding: вҖңIf they rule against our Country on Birthright Citizenship, which they probably will, it will be even worse, if thatвҖҷs possible. It will cost America massive amounts of money but, more importantly, it will cost America its DIGNITY!вҖқ

That ship may already have sailed.

Districts blow: The Virginia redistricting referendum was deemed invalid by a Trump-aligned state judge, who ruled the proposed amendment for new maps unconstitutional. Virginia Attorney General Jay Jones appealed.

Senate GOP move: In an overnight session, Senate Republicans pushed through a budget plan with a $70 billion increase for TrumpвҖҷs immigration enforcement вҖ“ the first step in the GOP effort to pass a filibuster-proof bill to reopen the Department of Homeland Security.

YouвҖҷre fired: Navy Secretary John Phelan was fired due to continued clashes with Pentagon leaders, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

Loss after loss: Trump Media CEO Devin Nunes announced his departure, providing no clear reason but with losses in the millions.

Political retaliation: The FBI investigated New York Times reporter Elizabeth Williamson over a piece about Director Kash Patel spending federal funds on his girlfriend. The probe aimed to accuse Williamson of breaking stalking laws.

Insider trading: The betting app Kalshi banned Virginia Senate candidate Mark Moran and congressional candidates Matt Klein and Ezekiel Enriquez after they were caught betting on their elections. Ironically, Klein co-wrote a bill to restrict prediction market bets.

Pardon me?: House Oversight Committee Republicans are seeking a pardon for Ghislaine Maxwell in an effort to secure her testimony in the Jeffrey Epstein affair.

In 2020, former House Intelligence Chair Devin Nunes lost a defamation suit against which imaginary animal?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israel killed at least five Palestinians, including three children, in a strike on civilians in northern Gaza, Al Jazeera reported, citing Palestinian officials.

рҹҮірҹҮө Gurung out: Nepal Minister of Home Affairs Sudan Gurung resigned, saying he wanted to ensure a proper investigation into his own investments, making him the second cabinet member to step down since the start of the new government.

рҹҮірҹҮ¬ Deadly raid: Around 20 people were killed in north-east Nigeria, in the villages of Pubagu and Mayo-Ladde, in an attack by suspected Boko Haram militants.

рҹҮ¬рҹҮ§ No more cigs: The British parliament passed a law to gradually raise the minimum age to buy cigarettes, thereby eventually banning smoking altogether.

These people arenвҖҷt just rewriting history or making up facts вҖ“ theyвҖҷre literally trying to reinvent math.

ZeteoвҖҷs Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

item.title: TrumpвҖҷs Omnishambles Deepens With Dire Strait News
item.description: On this day in 1937, eager law student Richard Milhous Nixon applied to be a special agent for the FBI. The application was unsuccessful. Years later, FBI director J Edgar Hoover would call the FBIвҖҷs loss вҖңultimately вҖҰ the countryвҖҷs gainвҖқ вҖ“ a judgment historians of the 37th president might beвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1937, eager law student Richard Milhous Nixon applied to be a special agent for the FBI. The application was unsuccessful. Years later, FBI director J Edgar Hoover would call the FBIвҖҷs loss вҖңultimately вҖҰ the countryвҖҷs gainвҖқ вҖ“ a judgment historians of the 37th president might be reluctant to accept.

Greetings, Martin here, honored to be your вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ author for the very first time. Before the main event, I want to point you to MehdiвҖҷs monologue on Pete HegsethвҖҷs Christian Crusade, aka the secretary of defenseвҖҷs justifications of war against a religious fundamentalist threat to world peace, expressed in the language of a religious fundamentalist threat to world peace. There is a terrifying Christian bigot in the Pentagon, and weвҖҷd all be better off if there werenвҖҷt.

Now to todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ in which the Pentagon gives Congress some very bad news indeed, a racist Supreme Court rant shows even Trump knows his own hand-picked justices are about to hand him his hat, and TrumpвҖҷs Navy secretary and media CEO are out.

On Wednesday, the Washington Post published a short report that highlighted a very big problem for Donald Trump as he tries to end his catastrophic war and arrest plummeting polling.

According to the Post, the Pentagon on Tuesday told Congress that clearing the Strait of Hormuz of Iranian mines could take six months вҖ“ and efforts to do so cannot start until the war is over.

The Strait is a key waterway for trade, particularly in oil, fertilizer, and other commodities. The Post is one of the mainstream outlets Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has tried to stop reporting on the Pentagon. HasnвҖҷt worked. Citing three sources, the paper said the timeline for clearing the Strait was вҖңmet with frustration by Democrats and Republicans alike,вҖқ as вҖңperhaps the clearest sign that gasoline and oil prices could remain elevated long after any peace deal is reached.вҖқ

Impervious to reality as ever, Trump insists Tehran will soon вҖң cry uncle.вҖқ But yesterday, as Iran attacked two cargo vessels in the Strait, a Nephew had ominous words for the White House.

Though mines in the Strait might not cause a вҖңtotal interruptionвҖқ of shipping traffic, said Richard Nephew, an Iran expert at Columbia University, вҖңYouвҖҷre not going to have many people wanting to run that riskвҖқ and send ships into death-strewn waters.

Furthermore, even if peace can be found and a six-month effort to clear the Strait then starts, it seems Iran wonвҖҷt be much help, given it has reportedly indicated it doesnвҖҷt know where all its mines are.

Behind every such headline lingers a dread fact for Trump: the midterm elections are just six months away. And every day, in every way, the administration is lurching further into the feared world of the вҖң omnishambles.вҖқ

Polling shows it. This week, before the bad news about the Strait and the mines, AP/NORC found TrumpвҖҷs approval on the economy at just 30%, down eight points from March, while 72% of Americans said the country was heading in the wrong direction. TrumpвҖҷs favorability rating was also down, by five points to 33%.

Nor is it just the war and the economy that are driving people away from Trump and the Republicans prostrated before him. Over at Reuters/IPSOS, a majority of respondents gave the thumbs down to TrumpвҖҷs hardline immigration policy.

Images of state brutality and murder may not have swamped our screens since January, when ICE ran amok in Minneapolis, but as Sarah вҖӢPierce, director of social policy for Third Way, told Reuters: вҖңPeople were being pulled out of cars, a priest shot with pepper balls, and Americans killed before our eyes. I donвҖҷt think those images are going to go away anytime soon.вҖң

NBC News found TrumpвҖҷs approval rating slumped to 37%, while nearly 70% of respondents disapproved of TrumpвҖҷs handling of inflation and the cost of living.

And amid it all, the Iran war drags on, with Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz.

To use words Trump might understand вҖ“ his own вҖ“ for his administration, this is вҖң Not good, not good at all.вҖң

Death tolls:

Iran: An Iranian official said this week that the death toll from US-Israeli strikes is at 3,375, including 383 children. It was unclear whether the death toll also included military and security forces.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,294 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to figures from the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 34 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war newsвҖҰ

Resolution rejected: The Senate rejected a war powers resolution to rein in TrumpвҖҷs war on Iran вҖ“ for a fifth time вҖ“ in a vote of 46 to 51.

Trump claim: The president said he prevented Iran from executing вҖңeight women protestersвҖқ вҖ“ but provided no further details. Iran said no such executions had been planned.

No relation: Two Iranian women held in the US are not related to the late military commander Qasem Soleimani as claimed by the Trump administration, Drop Site News reported, adding that one of the women is now seriously ill in a Texas ICE facility.

Israel kills another journalist: Journalist Amal Khalil was killed in southern Lebanon by Israeli strikes after getting trapped under rubble and being unable to access emergency services, as IsraelвҖҷs continued attacks blocked rescuers.

It seems Donald Trump knows the Supreme Court is going to rule against him again, over his laughable attempt to end birthright citizenship as enshrined by the 14th Amendment since 1868.

вҖңHow can the Democrats not like how the US Supreme Court votes,вҖқ the president began a raving madmanвҖҷs screed вҖ“ sorry, вҖңpostвҖқ вҖ“ on Wednesday. вҖңThe Democrat Justices stick together like glueвҖҰ [and] ALWAYS vote as a group, or BLOCK, even that new, Low IQ person, that somehow found her way to the bench.вҖқ

That was a vile, racist, and misogynistic reference to Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, the double Harvard grad and esteemed judge appointed by Joe Biden in 2022.

Ranting on, Trump claimed, вҖңthe Republican Justices donвҖҷt stick together, they give the Democrats win after win.вҖқ It was, of course, nonsense, about a court with three Trump picks among six right-wingers who mostly rule in his favor and who helped him return to power, bestowing broad immunity and rejecting attempts to disqualify him on constitutional grounds.

Regardless, Trump called the courtвҖҷs recent rejection of his tariffs вҖңan unnecessary and expensive slap in the face to the USA,вҖқ adding: вҖңIf they rule against our Country on Birthright Citizenship, which they probably will, it will be even worse, if thatвҖҷs possible. It will cost America massive amounts of money but, more importantly, it will cost America its DIGNITY!вҖқ

That ship may already have sailed.

Districts blow: The Virginia redistricting referendum was deemed invalid by a Trump-aligned state judge, who ruled the proposed amendment for new maps unconstitutional. Virginia Attorney General Jay Jones appealed.

Senate GOP move: In an overnight session, Senate Republicans pushed through a budget plan with a $70 billion increase for TrumpвҖҷs immigration enforcement вҖ“ the first step in the GOP effort to pass a filibuster-proof bill to reopen the Department of Homeland Security.

YouвҖҷre fired: Navy Secretary John Phelan was fired due to continued clashes with Pentagon leaders, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

Loss after loss: Trump Media CEO Devin Nunes announced his departure, providing no clear reason but with losses in the millions.

Political retaliation: The FBI investigated New York Times reporter Elizabeth Williamson over a piece about Director Kash Patel spending federal funds on his girlfriend. The probe aimed to accuse Williamson of breaking stalking laws.

Insider trading: The betting app Kalshi banned Virginia Senate candidate Mark Moran and congressional candidates Matt Klein and Ezekiel Enriquez after they were caught betting on their elections. Ironically, Klein co-wrote a bill to restrict prediction market bets.

Pardon me?: House Oversight Committee Republicans are seeking a pardon for Ghislaine Maxwell in an effort to secure her testimony in the Jeffrey Epstein affair.

In 2020, former House Intelligence Chair Devin Nunes lost a defamation suit against which imaginary animal?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israel killed at least five Palestinians, including three children, in a strike on civilians in northern Gaza, Al Jazeera reported, citing Palestinian officials.

рҹҮірҹҮө Gurung out: Nepal Minister of Home Affairs Sudan Gurung resigned, saying he wanted to ensure a proper investigation into his own investments, making him the second cabinet member to step down since the start of the new government.

рҹҮірҹҮ¬ Deadly raid: Around 20 people were killed in north-east Nigeria, in the villages of Pubagu and Mayo-Ladde, in an attack by suspected Boko Haram militants.

рҹҮ¬рҹҮ§ No more cigs: The British parliament passed a law to gradually raise the minimum age to buy cigarettes, thereby eventually banning smoking altogether.

These people arenвҖҷt just rewriting history or making up facts вҖ“ theyвҖҷre literally trying to reinvent math.

ZeteoвҖҷs Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

item.title: The Truth Behind That Tucker Carlson Apology Video
item.description: Good evening, and welcome to this weekвҖҷs вҖҳRagebait.вҖҷ Justin Baragona here, bringing you the latest media dispatches from the right-wing industrial outrage complex. In tonightвҖҷs edition, we take a deep dive into Tucker CarlsonвҖҷs mea culpa for вҖңmisleading peopleвҖқ into voting for Trump вҖ“ and whyвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Good evening, and welcome to this weekвҖҷs вҖҳRagebait.вҖҷ

Justin Baragona here, bringing you the latest media dispatches from the right-wing industrial outrage complex. In tonightвҖҷs edition, we take a deep dive into Tucker CarlsonвҖҷs mea culpa for вҖңmisleading peopleвҖқ into voting for Trump вҖ“ and why progressives shouldnвҖҷt just take it at face value. Plus, we ask: with TrumpвҖҷs approval numbers sinking to new lows, is it just a matter of time before Fox abandons him?

You do not, under any circumstances, got to hand it to Tucker Carlson.

The former Fox star has increasingly drawn plaudits from progressives for his вҖңcredible anti-war voiceвҖқ and outspoken criticism of IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza, resulting in Donald Trump unceremoniously excommunicating his one-time close ally and adviser from MAGA.

With Democratic lawmakers talking up a potential bipartisan alliance that would feature Carlson, the far-right pundit sparked even more applause from the left when he issued an apology for вҖңmisleading peopleвҖқ with his past advocacy for Trump.

вҖңYou know, weвҖҷll be tormented by it for a long time вҖ“ I will beвҖҰ It was not intentional,вҖқ Carlson declared at the tail-end of a two-plus-hour podcast conversation with his brother, former Trump speechwriter Buckley Carlson, whom he colloquially referred to as вҖңUncle Buck.вҖқ

вҖңYou and I and everyone else who supported him вҖ“ you wrote speeches for him, I campaigned for him вҖ“ I mean, weвҖҷre implicated in this for sure,вҖқ Tucker Carlson said. вҖңItвҖҷs not enough to say, вҖҳWell, I changed my mindвҖҷ вҖ“ or like, вҖҳOh, this is bad вҖ“ IвҖҷm out.вҖҷвҖқ

To be certain, TuckerвҖҷs explicit condemnation of TrumpвҖҷs illegal war against Iran and IsraelвҖҷs genocidal violence against Palestinians should be commended. It is clearly a good thing, and we can only hope that more on the right follow his lead on these particular issues.

However, I watched the entire two-plus-hour conversation, and it was readily apparent that the two had become disenchanted with Trump precisely because they believed he wasnвҖҷt extreme and fascistic enough, and they felt the president didnвҖҷt make good on his vows of retribution against liberals and Democrats.

Besides drawing copious headlines that hyped his mea culpa for helping Trump get elected, prominent left-wing personalities praised Carlson. Even Mehdi вҖ“ who also complimented Carlson recently for challenging an interviewer to define the phrase вҖңIsraelвҖҷs right to existвҖқ вҖ“ briefly gave Carlson some kudos. That is, until he listened to the rest of the conversation that took place between the two ultra-conservative brothers.

вҖңSorry, I screwed up late last night by posting a clip in which Tucker Carlson finally apologized for backing Trump, but I didnвҖҷt hear the disgusting language used by the other speaker,вҖқ Mehdi tweeted on Tuesday.

Indeed, shortly after Carlson expressed how torn up he was for supporting Trump, вҖңUncle BuckвҖқ chimed in with some conspiracy mongering about the Trump assassination attempts before referring to former Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris as, and I am not making this up, вҖңCackling Cameltoe.вҖқ

The thing is, that wasnвҖҷt the first time Buckley had used that grossly misogynistic slur when talking about the ex-veep during the chat with his brother.

It was also just the tip of the iceberg when it came to the rank homophobia, racism, xenophobia, anti-vax sentiment, conspiracy theories, and just outright repulsive behavior on display in the podcast.

Asked what his breaking point with Trump was, Buckley said it wasnвҖҷt just the Iran war, but also the failure of Trump to properly вҖңhold to accountвҖқ all of the right-wingвҖҷs boogeymen from the past decade of ragebait, which included: COVID vaccines, January 6, the George Floyd protests, Joe BidenвҖҷs preemptive pardons, Biden family corruption, the Russia probe, the autopen вҖңscandal,вҖқ and Barack Obama being вҖңguilty of treason,вҖқ among others.

вҖңIt is an offense against you and me and everyone we know,вҖқ Buckley fumed.

The two siblings also seemed obsessed with how вҖңsuper gayвҖқ Washington is, all while tossing out wild accusations about the sexual orientations of Hillary Clinton, Barack Obama, Lindsey Graham, and HarrisвҖҷs husband Doug Emhoff. According to the pair, this was all proof of the вҖңdemonic influenceвҖқ that inflicts powerful politicians, which they linked to homosexuality and secret вҖңsodomitesвҖқ in DC.

And there was more. So much more. Here is just a small, non-comprehensive sampling of what else was said:

вҖңIt was not his race to lose, it was ours вҖ“ and he did it on our behalf,вҖқ Tucker on John McCain.

вҖңHe hates whites,вҖқ Tucker on Obama.

вҖңYou didnвҖҷt take the vax. Thank god!вҖқ Tucker to Buckley.

вҖңNo wonder doctors are the worst people in America,вҖқ Tucker said, talking about doctors taking mRNA vaccines. He also called COVID vaccines вҖңpoisonвҖқ and a вҖңbioweaponвҖқ that вҖңkilled hundreds of thousands of peopleвҖқ around the world, and how Trump took the vaccine and still hasnвҖҷt apologized for doing that.

вҖңTheyвҖҷre representative of America and Americans. The best kind of people,вҖқ Buckley on the January 6 insurrectionists.

вҖңIt was an operation to get rid of white cops,вҖқ Tucker said on the George Floyd protests. The pair also bemoaned Trump for failing to вҖңexercise his powerвҖқ to quell the riots and free the вҖңwrongfully imprisonedвҖқ Derek Chauvin, the former police officer convicted of murdering Floyd.

Perhaps with his reprehensible views вҖ“ which are the same as they ever were вҖ“ laid out like this, progressives will think twice before looking to join forces with Tucker.

As Nicole Hemmer wrote for MS NOW earlier this month, вҖңshining a laudatory spotlight on CarlsonвҖқ and other MAGA conspiracists who have split with Trump вҖңdoes their work for them, lending their reputations new legitimacy the next time they attack immigrants or promote authoritarians.вҖқ

But Cenk Uygur, the founder of The Young Turks, offered a counterargument this week amid rising backlash from some on the left over making common cause with Tucker.

вҖңEveryone rejecting @TuckerCarlsonвҖҷs apology for backing Trump - what the fuck else do you want? If you donвҖҷt think itвҖҷs good enough that he rejected Trump and joined the anti-war, anti-genocide side, youвҖҷre a bit of an asshole,вҖқ he tweeted on Tuesday.

And after Mehdi brought up the вҖңhorribleвҖқ positions Tucker endorsed throughout the latest podcast, noting вҖңsome of us canвҖҷt get behindвҖқ that, Uygur pushed back: вҖңDid I miss the requirement to agree with all of his positions in order to accept his help in combating war and genocide?вҖқ

The truth is that when it comes to Tucker Carlson, weвҖҷve all вҖңseen this movie before,вҖқ as CNNвҖҷs Andrew Kirell noted on Tuesday, pointing out that the Dominion case revealed Carlson privately said in 2021 that he hated Trump вҖңpassionately.вҖқ However, by 2024, not only was Tucker fully back in TrumpвҖҷs inner circle, but he was also one of the driving forces behind JD VanceвҖҷs selection as running mate.

In the end, this is all just a calculated approach by Carlson to not only get ahead of the rest of the right-wing media ecosystem in distancing themselves from an increasingly unpopular president, but also to elevate and rebrand himself as the leader of a new post-Trump conservative movement.

On the other end of the MAGA media spectrum from Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly, Alex Jones, and the rest of the new anti-Trump right sits Fox.

The conservative cable giant has steadfastly remained in the presidentвҖҷs corner even as TrumpвҖҷs approval ratings are in freefall amid economic concerns and his illegal war of choice in Iran. Of course, it was TrumpвҖҷs вҖҳFox News CabinetвҖҷ who helped push the commander-in-chief to attack Iran in the first place.

Still, with just 32% of Americans approving of TrumpвҖҷs job performance вҖ“ which is approaching late-stage George W. Bush lows вҖ“ I wondered if we were getting close to the breaking point for Fox, especially if the midterm elections are a complete wipeout for the GOP.

Also, it isnвҖҷt as if we havenвҖҷt seen the Rupert Murdoch-owned network вҖ“ which has long served as the de facto communications arm of the Republican Party вҖ“ try to dump Trump before. Following his 2020 election loss, Fox repeatedly tried to move on from Trump, even enacting a вҖңsoft banвҖқ amid the Dominion lawsuit over the election denialism they peddled on his behalf.

Additionally, itвҖҷs no secret that Murdoch has long had a love-hate relationship with the president and tried desperately to find a new GOP standardbearer ahead of the 2024 election, to no avail. (Things have grown only more strained between Murdoch and Trump following the presidentвҖҷs lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal.)

Anyway, to get to the bottom of this, I decided to ask one of the only people I know who has watched more Fox News than I have over the past decade вҖ“ Media Matters senior fellow Matt Gertz.

вҖңI think it would take a seismic change in the polling, the likes of which we have not seen yet, because even as he is incredibly unpopular, Trump remains above 80, sometimes 85% among Republicans,вҖқ Gertz told me. вҖңThose are your hardcore Fox viewersвҖҰ and that is the thing that keeps the network from pivoting, and thatвҖҷs what weвҖҷve seen again and again over the years.вҖқ

Gertz pointed out that when Fox initially вҖңthought he was toastвҖқ after his 2020 election defeat and tried to move on, many of their viewers revolted and began ditching the network for more Trump-friendly options like Newsmax, forcing Fox to вҖңpivot really hard into election-denial stuffвҖқ to win them back.

вҖңAnd you know, that ended up costing them $787 million from the Dominion settlement. But I think my read is that they viewed it as cheap at that price because they get to keep the viewers coming back,вҖқ he added.

Another point Gertz raised is that over the past few years, Fox has largely rid itself of Trump-skeptical voices, so the on-air talent willing to consistently push back against the president is largely absent.

вҖңThey purged the news side, and all the Chris Wallace, Shep Smith, Neil Cavuto types have been pushed out,вҖқ Gertz said. вҖңSteve Doocy became basically the most prominent sometimes-skeptic after that, and heвҖҷs been put out to pasture.вҖқ

In other words, the bottom line is that we likely wonвҖҷt see Fox distance itself from Trump any time soon, as it is fully captured by its audience.

вҖңThey recognize that they had basically had a near-death experience after the 2020 election, and what they learned from that was, even if it requires them to tell lies that are incredibly expensive in the realm of hundreds of millions of dollars, itвҖҷs in their interest to do that,вҖқ Gertz added.

вҖңTheyвҖҷre in so deep at this point,вҖқ he concluded. вҖңTheyвҖҷve defended him through multiple impeachments, an insurrection, and incredible amounts of corruption over the past year. TheyвҖҷre staffing the administration. Sean Hannity retains a fairly powerful post. Laura IngrahamвҖҷs in business with Donald Trump Jr. I mean, theyвҖҷre just in too deep at this point.вҖқ

A Fox News representative did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Watch out for my newsletter, вҖҳRagebait,вҖҷ every week. I focus on the right-wing outrage-industrial complex and the intersection of our political and media landscapes. Send any tips on Signal (justinbaragona.85).

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: Warnings For Americans From A Historian of the Nazis
item.description: Donald TrumpвҖҷs musings about dictatorship once sounded almost funny. Nobody laughs anymore. Trump has not created a dictatorship. He has, however, dragged the US further toward authoritarian rule than many of us thought possible. His corruption of the legal system, subordination of Congress,вҖҰ
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
Donald TrumpвҖҷs musings about dictatorship once sounded almost funny. Nobody laughs anymore.

Trump has not created a dictatorship. He has, however, dragged the US further toward authoritarian rule than many of us thought possible. His corruption of the legal system, subordination of Congress, embrace of state violence, and hostility toward free elections led the sober-minded centrist Jonathan Rauch to write earlier this year: вҖңYes, itвҖҷs fascism.вҖқ

How Trump has managed to do that, contravening the values that made the US the worldвҖҷs leading democracy, remains hard to fathom. But at least part of the answer lies in his use of techniques that earlier historical figures have used to amass and centralize power.

Laurence Rees, a former head of history at the BBC and best-selling author, has made studying those techniques his careerвҖҷs work. His books and video documentaries explore how skillful, if repugnant, Nazi leadership drove ordinary Germans to monstrous wrongdoing during World War II. In January 2025, as Trump returned to the White House, Rees published вҖҳThe Nazi MindвҖҷ in an attempt to explain the inexplicable.

To state the obvious, Donald Trump is not Adolf Hitler. MAGA is not Nazism. No despot or movement in history compares to the Third Reich, and the nature and scale of evil it perpetrated.

Moreover, TrumpвҖҷs handiwork appears less formidable today. Most of America recoils from his second-term grotesquerie. If permitted free and fair elections, voters will punish his Republican Party this fall вҖ“ just as Hungarian voters have now punished TrumpвҖҷs authoritarian role model Viktor OrbГЎn.

But the lessons Rees identifies remain relevant to the crisis confronting 21st-century America. And that is not merely because Trump and a disturbing number of his young fans have at times expressed admiration for Nazi strength and ruthlessness.

вҖңEverything is fragile вҖ“ often a great deal more fragile than we think,вҖқ Rees writes. He lists 12 вҖңwarning signsвҖқ of how authoritarians can corrupt democracy.

All sound familiar.

Spreading conspiracy theories. Nazis persuaded rank-and-file Germans that their problems stemmed from the machinations of Jews. You can hear unmistakable echoes as Trump factotums blame immigrants for violent crime, stolen tax money, even high housing costs. вҖңThe mass looting of a civilization,вҖқ spits White House propagandist Stephen Miller.

Using вҖңthemвҖқ and вҖңus.вҖқ Would-be dictators rouse supporters by exploiting their primal instinct to sort friends from foes, scapegoating those who seem different. TrumpвҖҷs rants and social media feeds show this.

Leading as a hero. Myths of heroism helped elevate authoritarian leaders from Napoleon to Hitler to Castro. Having created an image of business success, Trump promotes fantasies about his personal qualities by invoking aspirations for the Nobel Peace Prize and Congressional Medal of Honor; he depicted himself as Jesus on Truth Social.

Deploying the photograph of Trump bloodied with a raised fist after a 2024 assassination attempt, he and his supporters have declared he was вҖңsaved by GodвҖқ to save America.

Corrupting youth. Those under 25, their brains not fully matured, show particular vulnerability to the lure of extremism. The conservative writer Rod Dreher has estimated that 30% or more of Gen Z Republican operatives identify with Groypers. Trump has sprinkled such Nazi-adjacent loons through his administration.

Andrew, a 20-year-old white Christian landscaper, told the conservative City Journal last year, вҖңHitler was a nationalist. He was like, we have to take Germany back for Germans, and I feel like we should do that for America.вҖқ

Conniving with the elite. вҖңDictators frequently do not seize control on their own,вҖқ Rees says. вҖңInstead, power is handed to them by others.вҖқ Among those empowering Trump: tech billionaires, Wall Street executives, Republican congressional leaders, the conservative-led Supreme Court.

Attacking human rights. Authoritarians make subverting the rule of law and curbing press freedom job number one. Trump and his administration have tried hard on multiple fronts, from threatening regulatory punishment of news networks to denying due process to arrested immigrants to ginning up baseless prosecutions of political adversaries.

Exploiting faith. Promises of a utopian future resembling an imaginary past represent a critical tool for rallying adherents. Thus Trump, behind a facade of religious inspiration, constantly predicts AmericaвҖҷs return to a вҖңgolden ageвҖқ that allegedly existed once upon a time.

Valuing enemies. Identification of hostile forces binds authoritarian movements together. Trump and MAGA use labels such as вҖңdomestic terrorists,вҖқ вҖңCommunists,вҖқ and вҖңradical left, country-hating Democrats.вҖқ

Eliminating resistance. Would-be dictators often face institutional opposition from the church and the military, among others. Trump seeks to cow the Vatican, while Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth weeds out generals for insufficient loyalty. The administration applies legal pressure to Democratic lawmakers and progressive organizations that challenge him.

Escalating racism. The modern GOP assembled its national majority with white conservatives repelled by the DemocratsвҖҷ embrace of civil rights. Trump and prominent followers have taken todayвҖҷs racial politics to another level by asserting the inherent superiority of white societies in the style of 20th-century eugenicists вҖ“ who included Hitler.

Killing at a distance. Violence up close is harder to commit than at physical remove. Trump and his aides revel in aerial strikes on boats in international waters or targets in Iran, as if playing video games.

Stoking fear. вҖңOne of the most potent political statements of all is, вҖҳBe frightened, they are coming for your homes and children,вҖҷвҖқ Rees concludes. Trump has sounded that theme since he rode down the golden escalator in 2015.

Trump has used that bracing playbook to damage our country and the world. But Rees concludes his book on a note of optimism, which should hearten those seeking to reclaim AmericaвҖҷs democratic heritage.

вҖңAdolf Hitler employed every one of the techniques above,вҖқ he writes. вҖңBut in the end вҖ“ at enormous human cost вҖ“ something tremendous happened. The Nazis were defeated.вҖқ

John Harwood is the former chief Washington correspondent for CNBC and White House correspondent for CNN. He has interviewed every president from George H.W. Bush to Joe Biden. Sign up for вҖҳThe Stakes with John HarwoodвҖҷ to get all of his columns in your inbox.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: Good News for Democrats вҖ“ and democrats
item.description: On this day in 1954, four years into the vicious and repressive вҖңRed ScareвҖқ in American politics, Senator Joseph McCarthy began hearings into supposed communist infiltration of the US Army. Televised, the hearings exposed the far-right Wisconsin Republican to public revulsion, ultimately causingвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1954, four years into the vicious and repressive вҖңRed ScareвҖқ in American politics, Senator Joseph McCarthy began hearings into supposed communist infiltration of the US Army. Televised, the hearings exposed the far-right Wisconsin Republican to public revulsion, ultimately causing his downfall. How times have changedвҖҰ

Good morning, friends. Prem here. Wishing you a wonderful Wednesday wherever you are. We have just published our long-awaited profile of Hasan Piker вҖ“В one of the most prominent left-wing commentators in the US who is now becoming one of the most targeted. I traveled to Los Angeles to spend time with Piker and gain a better understanding of him, beneath all the outrage. The result was a deep, meticulous, and fair profile you will not find anywhere else. Read it here.

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ Virginia voters punch back against the GOP in the redistricting arms race, another Democratic candidate flip-flops on IsraelвҖҷs genocide, and Republicans desperately try whipping up another faux-outrage cycle while the Trump administration fumbles Iran war negotiationsвҖҰ again.

On Tuesday night, Virginians voted to approve a redistricting measure that will likely help Democrats flip multiple Republican-held seats in the upcoming midterms.

The measure, which voters passed by a roughly two-point margin, will, if approved by the state legislature a second time, allow lawmakers to redraw congressional districts before the midterm elections in November. The redrawn maps may shift VirginiaвҖҷs congressional delegation from 6-5 to a whopping 10-1 in favor of Democrats.

Nearly $100 million of largely dark money was spent on the election, much of it in support of the measure.

The new maps are part of an array of redistricting battles set off nationwide last year, after President Donald Trump urged Republican states to carve out more GOP-friendly districts in order to hold onto the partyвҖҷs narrow House majority. Texas, Missouri, and North Carolina answered TrumpвҖҷs call, while Ohio was already in the process of creating new maps that will likely favor Republicans even more.

California legislators and voters punched back, creating new maps that could at least counter new Republican seats in Texas.

Florida is up next. Governor Ron DeSantis has invoked a special legislative session this month, focused on redistricting. After Tuesday nightвҖҷs result, House Democratic Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries issued a warning to DeSantis.

вҖңThis war is not over. Next week, Ron DeSantis is hauling the Florida legislature back into a special session to redraw maps because Republicans know they are on the verge of an epic defeat in November,вҖқ Jeffries said in a statement.

вҖңIf Florida Republicans proceed with this illegal scheme, they will only create more prime pick-up opportunities for Democrats, just as they did with TrumpвҖҷs dummymander in Texas.вҖқ

Jeffries said his party вҖңwill aggressively target for defeatвҖқ Florida Republicans Mario DГӯaz-Balart, Maria Elvira Salazar, Carlos GimГ©nez, Kat Cammack, Anna Paulina Luna, Laurel Lee, Cory Mills, and Brian Mast.

вҖңWe are prepared to take them all on, and we are prepared to win,вҖқ he concluded. вҖңMaximum warfare, everywhere, all the time.вҖқ

As it stands, Republicans are in deep trouble. Generic ballot averages have them down by between five and six points, and thereвҖҷs no sign of conditions improving for the party as the top of the ticket continues to depress enthusiasm.

Four polls released Tuesday showed Trump hitting or tying second-term lows.

The American Research Group showed Trump at a dismal 32% approval вҖ“ among the lowest approval ratings a US president has ever had, and the lowest the poll has shown Trump hitting in either term.

Conditions are so bad for Republicans that the Senate is in play despite what was previously seen as an unfavorable set of elections for Democrats. Democrats are now slightly favored in North Carolina and Georgia, while Ohio, Nebraska, Iowa, and even Texas are in the conversation.

Everything the GOP appeared to think it would own upon TrumpвҖҷs return вҖ“ from immigration and the economy, to foreign policy and the Epstein files вҖ“ has instead turned horribly toxic for the party, due to its own flailing approach.

In response, the party is not adapting at all, or trying to earn back the publicвҖҷs trust. Instead, it is urgently pursuing radical voter suppression proposals like the so-called вҖңSAVE America Act,вҖқ and flirting with sending masked ICE agents to terrorize and intimidate people at the polls.

Republicans are also frantically trying to whip up outrage cycles, focused, for example, on the Twitch streamer Hasan Piker, or by pretending that a sarcastic tweet from Senator Chris Murphy was cheerleading for Iran.

Republicans have no juice in the polls. They are flopping on every single issue (including ones like immigration, which they had assumed were among their crutch issues!). ItвҖҷs no wonder that the only initiative they have left is to try stealing an election once again.

Death tolls:

Iran: An Iranian official said this week that the death toll from US-Israeli strikes is at 3,375, including 383 children. It was unclear whether the death toll also included military and security forces.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,294 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to figures from the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 34 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war newsвҖҰ

IRGC attacks ships: IranвҖҷs Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps seized at least two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, state media reported. The IRGC said the ships вҖңoperated without authorization, repeatedly violated regulations, manipulated navigational aid systems and sought to covertly exit the Strait of Hormuz, endangering maritime security.вҖқ AP, citing semiofficial Iranian media, reported that Iran attacked a third ship later Wednesday.

Talks talk: Amid uncertainty that ultimately stopped JD Vance from traveling to Pakistan, the New York Times reported that Iranian officials simply donвҖҷt trust Donald Trump, arising from his decision to rip up the nuclear deal in 2018.

Ceasefire extended: Trump announced an extension at the request of Pakistan, until Tehran вҖңcome[s] up with a unified proposalвҖқ and negotiations are over. Trump is continuing to order a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

Senate storm: The right-wing outrage machine revved up over a post in which Senator Chris Murphy wrote вҖңawesomeвҖқ over news of Iranian ships beating the blockade. вҖңI guess I just have to be more careful about sarcasm on Twitter,вҖқ the Democrat told Fox.

Lebanon strikes: Hezbollah said it fired rockets and drones into northern Israel, citing ceasefire violations by Israeli forces, which the Lebanese group said hit civilians and destroyed homes. Israel said it hit the rocket launcher and intercepted a drone. Al Jazeera reported that Israel continued to hit residential areas in southern Lebanon today.

Statue sentences: Two Israeli soldiers were jailed for 30 days for smashing a sculpture of Jesus in a Christian village in southern Lebanon, an act that caused worldwide revulsion. Israel also replaced the statue.

Jasmeet Bains, a state lawmaker running for Congress in California, has been endorsed by Democratic Majority for Israel, a pro-Israel super PAC. Now, sheвҖҷs walking back her previous comment acknowledging that Israel has committed genocide in Gaza.

On a Zoom call with Fresno County Young Democrats earlier this year, Bains was asked simply: вҖңIs Israel committing genocide in Gaza?вҖқ She responded: вҖңYes.вҖқ

This week, Bains denied the genocide, with her campaign telling Politico she misunderstood the question.

вҖңI approach the word вҖҳgenocideвҖҷ with care, and I donвҖҷt believe it applies to Israel,вҖқ Bains said in a statement. вҖңWe need leaders in Congress who approach these issues with the seriousness and thoughtfulness they deserve вҖ“ not soundbites or false choices вҖ“ and that will continue to be my focus.вҖқ

Bains is running against Randy Villegas, who acknowledges IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza. He has been endorsed by Bernie Sanders.

Pure betrayal: Trump is considering sending more than 1,000 Afghans who worked for the US during the war in their country but have been denied resettlement to the Democratic Republic of Congo, the New York Times reported.

Boat strike: Ecuadorean fishermen described to The Guardian their horrific experience after a US drone attack, including their arrest, maltreatment, removal to El Salvador, and release without charge, amid strikes supposedly aimed at drug runners that have killed nearly 200.

Political prosecution: The Southern Poverty Law Center, which monitors far-right groups, was indicted on federal fraud charges related to payments to informants. Long a target of Republican attacks, the SPLC denies wrongdoing.

Da Do Ron, Don: Trump says Ron DeSantis is вҖңbeggingвҖқ for a job, according to Axios. The Florida governor, thrashed by Trump in 2024, reportedly has his eye on attorney general, defense secretary, or even - donвҖҷt laugh -a seat on the Supreme Court.

Dem out: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida quit the US House, heading off motions to expel over alleged misuse of federal funds, which she denies.

Pac man: AIPACвҖҷs super PAC is spending nearly $800,000 on ads targeting Republican Thomas Massie for voting with liberal Democrats on Israel.

What, in 2023, was Florida Governor Ron DeSantis forced to say he could not recall doing on a 2019 flight from Tallahassee to Washington, DC?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israel killed seven Palestinians across the Gaza Strip yesterday, including a child in Jabalia Camp. Several were injured, Palestinian media reported. Israel killed another man in Jabalia today. Meanwhile, in the occupied West Bank, Israeli forces reportedly fired on mourners attending the funeral of two Palestinians killed in yesterdayвҖҷs settler attack on a Palestinian school.

рҹҮірҹҮ¬ Coup attempt: The Nigerian government charged six people for allegedly plotting to overthrow President Bola Tinubu, only a few months after a coup was said to have been thwarted. A retired general and a police inspector are among the suspects.

рҹҮӘрҹҮәрҹҮӯрҹҮә Legal victory: The European Court of Justice struck down a Hungarian law banning вҖңpromotion of homosexualityвҖқ to minors.

рҹҮҜрҹҮө Arms sales: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said Japan will lift a decades-long ban on exporting lethal weapons, as it moves away from pacifist policies. (Nothing at all to be afraid of, right?)

рҹҮөрҹҮӘ Corvetto out: Peru election chief Piero Corvetto resigned amid delayed results from last weekвҖҷs election, hoping to вҖңgenerate a climate of greater confidence.вҖқ


It is truly insane that the man who wants to be, and who Trump wants to be, the next chair of the Federal Reserve cannot say who lost the 2020 election. IT IS A CULT.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

item.title: A Palestinian School Day Ended With Israeli SettlersвҖҷ Gunfire. A Boy Was Killed, Just as His Father Was 7 Years Ago
item.description: This article was published in collaboration with Egab. AL MUGHAYYIR, the occupied West Bank вҖ“ The morning at Al-Mughayyir BoysвҖҷ Secondary School had begun the way school mornings do. Two hundred students between the ages of 6 and 17 were in their classrooms, teachers at their boards, notebooksвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
This article was published in collaboration with Egab.

AL MUGHAYYIR, the occupied West Bank вҖ“ The morning at Al-Mughayyir BoysвҖҷ Secondary School had begun the way school mornings do. Two hundred students between the ages of 6 and 17 were in their classrooms, teachers at their boards, notebooks open. At around noon, the sound that ended it was not the bell.

The first shot came without warning. Within seconds, students were throwing themselves behind desks, pressing against walls, or freezing where they stood. Teachers moved fast, locking doors, pulling children away from windows, turning classrooms into holding rooms where the only lesson was staying still and quiet.

Principal Bassam Abu Assaf had dealt with settler incursions before. Groups arriving with clubs, threats, intimidation.

вҖңThis was the first time there was direct gunfire,вҖқ he tells me. вҖңThey intended to harm the students. The fire was direct and heavy.вҖқ His first instinct was not to call for help but to get word to parents. A voice message went out through messaging apps: Come now. Take your children.

Abu Assaf says he feared calling for help first would take too long, time that could put the students in even more danger. Unfortunately, he could not know what was waiting for students outside the school when they tried to reach their parents.

The school sits on the edge of Al-Mughayyir village, east of Ramallah in the occupied West Bank, surrounded by open land, exposed on all sides. Three settlers, accompanied by three Israeli soldiers, according to witness accounts, had positioned themselves near the western wall вҖ“ a two-meter (6.5-foot) concrete barrier topped by another two meters of mesh вҖ“ and were firing toward the building and the courtyard. When parents began arriving, and students poured out to find them. ThatвҖҷs when the shooting intensified. The courtyard, meant to be the escape route, became the killing ground.

Aws Hamdi Al-Naasan was 14 years old and in ninth grade. He did not make it across the courtyard. He did not reach the gate.

His fatherвҖҷs uncle, Faraj Naasan, watched from the window of his house overlooking the school. He heard the shots, saw the chaos, and understood what was happening before he could fully process it. He had seen something like this before, not as a distant news event, but in his own family.

AwsвҖҷs father, Hamdi, was killed by Israeli settlers in 2019. He had gone out during a settler attack to help carry the wounded, managed to evacuate two injured people, and was shot while trying to reach a third. Now his son had been killed in the same village, in the same kind of attack, more than seven years later.

вҖңThis is a living embodiment of history repeating itself,вҖқ Faraj says.

Also killed in the school attack on Tuesday was Jihad Marzouq Abu Naiz, a 32-year-old resident of the village who had rushed to the school when he heard what was happening, trying to help get students out. He had left work the moment word spread. He did not come back.

Five students were wounded, some critically, according to the Palestinian Health Ministry. Ambulances could not get through the chaos quickly enough, and parents loaded injured children into their own cars. At the hospital, Aws was declared dead. Minutes later, so was Jihad.

Inside the school, a teacher from Al Naasan family, who asked not to give his full name, says what happened was вҖңbeyond what any school can bear.вҖқ

вҖңThe children who walked out today,вҖқ he tells me, вҖңcarried something with them that would not leave quickly. This trauma will live with them forever.вҖқ

Abu Assaf, recounting the morning, kept returning to the same word: the fire was direct. Not stray, not incidental. вҖңThey intended to harm the students,вҖқ he says again. In 22 years of teaching, this was not something he had prepared for.

Inside the school, blood stained the courtyard, notebooks were left open on desks. Chairs were still pushed back from where students had scrambled and run. But the violence in the occupied West Bank didnвҖҷt stop at the school.

PalestineвҖҷs Foreign Ministry condemned in the strongest terms, describing the assault on Al-Mughayyir school as a massacre carried out by settler gangs вҖңin full coordination with the occupation army.вҖқ

вҖңIsraelвҖҷs continued contempt for the international consensus rejecting settler-colonialism, and the absence of any real accountability, encourages further crimes against our people and reflects the international communityвҖҷs inability to impose its will in applying international law,вҖқ the ministry said.

The Israeli military claimed, without evidence, that a reservist had opened fire near the area on individuals who were throwing rocks toward the vehicle he was in, according to Israeli media. Soldiers were then deployed to the area. It added that it was investigating the incident and вҖңits findings will be forwarded to the authorities.вҖқ As Palestinians know all too well, such investigations almost never produce any prosecutions, and settlers and Israeli soldiers act with impunity.

With the deaths of Al-Naasan and Abu Naim, the toll in the occupied West Bank rose to four killed by Israeli violence since morning. The Palestinian Ministry of Health also announced the death of Mohammed Majdi Al-Jaabari, 16, struck by a settler vehicle in Hebron, and Rajaa Fadl Beitawi, 49, who died from gunshot wounds sustained during an Israeli forces raid in Jenin approximately two years ago.

According to the Wall and Settlement Resistance Commission, Israeli forces and settlers carried out a combined 1,819 attacks in March alone вҖ“ 1,322 by Israeli forces and 497 by settlers. The attacks were concentrated in Hebron governorate with 321 incidents, followed by Nablus with 315, Ramallah and Al-Bireh with 292, and Jerusalem with 203 вҖ“ figures the commission described as вҖңevidence of systematic, concentrated targeting across the West BankвҖқ.

The village of Al-Mughayyir has seen repeated settler attacks in recent years. For the school, until now, the violence had stopped at the gate. This morning it did not, and a boy who had grown up without his father died the same way his father did, in the same dust, under the same sky.

Aseel Mafarjeh is a West Bank-focused journalist based in Ramallah, focusing on local stories that speak of the challenges and creativity of youth in Palestine.
