item.title: Inside the Heart and Mind of Hasan Piker
item.description: A note from our Editor-in-Chief: Prem traveled to Los Angeles to speak with and shadow Hasan Piker, one of the biggest, most controversial leftist voices in the US, who streams to hundreds of thousands of people every day on the platform Twitch. We sought to get a deeper understanding of the manвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
A note from our Editor-in-Chief:

Prem traveled to Los Angeles to speak with and shadow Hasan Piker, one of the biggest, most controversial leftist voices in the US, who streams to hundreds of thousands of people every day on the platform Twitch. We sought to get a deeper understanding of the man who has recently been thrust into the center of American politics.

This is an important and lengthy piece from Prem that we here at Zeteo believe reveals deeper insights into a progressive voice that the mainstream media, the Democratic establishment, and, of course, the Republican Party have relentlessly smeared and demonized. Please do subscribe to Zeteo for full access to this fascinating profile of Hasan Piker, and to help us do more independent journalism like this.

вҖ“ Mehdi

At the turn of the century, 9-year-old Hasan Piker sat at his classroom desk in Ankara, Turkey, giving his teacher a headache. He was hyperactive, energetic, and disinterested in simply copying down what the teacher instructed him to. He liked to push back and argue. All of his teachers would have a field day with his mother, whenever they saw her.

вҖңI was an annoying little shithead,вҖқ he tells me. ItвҖҷs a moniker some of his opponents might say still applies more than a quarter century later.

He reminisced on his childhood as we sat in his LA home studio вҖ“ a lair brimming with politics and gaming and culture and graphic art, all backdropping where he streams to hundreds of thousands around the world.

Back in the third grade in Turkey, Piker recalls, his provocative nature came out during a religion class. вҖңI remember debating my teacher,вҖқ he says. вҖңI was like, вҖҳWhy are we only learning about Islam, if this is religion class? Why are we not learning about any other religions?вҖҷвҖқ He bristled at what felt like вҖңindoctrination.вҖқ And so PikerвҖҷs relationship to faith began to develop into what it is today, two decades later: вҖңnon-existent,вҖқ he says, beyond identifying as culturally Muslim.

His вҖңcontractвҖқ with a higher power, if one exists at all, is to just pursue good, he says, musing that if there is an omnipotent being, they likely wonвҖҷt punish someone who tried to do their best, but also consumed pork or alcohol. Not that heвҖҷs trying to do good for rewards in the afterlife, he notes.

But PikerвҖҷs childhood streak for challenging conventions persists. So much so that the entire US political establishment is now targeting him, depicting him as a radical extremist and terrorist sympathizer.

Today, Piker is one of the most prominent leftist political commentators in the US, and streams to hundreds of thousands of people every day on the platform Twitch. He has become a lightning rod вҖ“ a functional Public Enemy Number One in the eyes of both major political parties.

As US-Israeli violence ravages Palestine, Lebanon, and Iran, cable networks like CNN and Fox have dedicated copious amounts of airtime to shaming this one streamer. CNN anchor Dana Bash falsely said that he excuses sexual violence. Republican politicians have followed suit. Florida Congressman Randy Fine called Piker a вҖңjihadist.вҖқ Vice President JD Vance compared him to cannibal serial killer Jeffrey Dahmer. Florida Senator Rick Scott has said Piker вҖңhates AmericaвҖқ and вҖңglorifies terrorism.вҖқ Democrats arenвҖҷt far behind. Illinois Congressman Brad Schneider called Piker an вҖңunapologetic antisemiteвҖқ while Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow compared Piker to the white supremacist, Holocaust-denying commentator Nick Fuentes.

Piker calls the comparison to Fuentes вҖңtotally indecent.вҖқ ItвҖҷs among the more common smears he said heвҖҷs received: вҖңbeing compared to an avowed neo-Nazi, white supremacist, whose worldview I find to be morally repugnant, whose opinions I have spent my entire life combating вҖ“ opinions just like that.вҖқ

In many ways, the cancellation campaign against Piker isnвҖҷt about what he believes, what heвҖҷs said, or what heвҖҷs meant. ItвҖҷs about marginalizing the left, and stymying the spread of growing anti-Israel sentiment. That doesnвҖҷt make the personal smears less painful, or the ensuing death threats less real.

None of that, though, is stopping him.

ItвҖҷs Saturday, and PikerвҖҷs set for another routine day of streaming. I arrive at his home slightly before him. His assistant buzzes me in through an entry gate. PikerвҖҷs dog, Kaya, bounds out to greet me, and leads me inside his swanky home.

The first thing I see upon entering is a large office that looks like a cable news commentatorвҖҷs studio meets comic book store meets Democratic Socialists of America (DSA). Political memorabilia is strewn everywhere, sewn between shelves of books and streaming awards and video games and basketballs and figurines and artistic renderings of Piker and Kaya.

Minutes later, the towering Piker walks in and greets me, briefly. He fires up his computer, opens the Twitch livestream waiting room, freshens up, and promptly returns back to his desk. вҖңWhatвҖҷs up folks, IвҖҷm live and alive,вҖқ he announces to his Instagram followers, his common refrain to welcome viewers into his livestream.

As people file in by the thousands, Piker begins by sharing snippets of his personal life for a few minutes. After he hosted progressive Rep. Ilhan Omar on his stream the previous evening, he says, he went straight to help host a screening of вҖҳThe Voice of Hind Rajab,вҖҷ a film about a child murdered in Gaza by Israeli forces. He celebrated his grandmotherвҖҷs birthday that morning, in lieu of working out.

вҖңThereвҖҷs not really too much going on in my life other than those things, cause as you already know, IвҖҷm a chud-ass loser,вҖқ he quips.

With split monitors, one eye is trained onto the chat; one hand monitors the chatвҖҷs pace, highlighting comments heвҖҷll respond to; another part of his brain toggles between serious political analysis, jokes, expletives, and historical recall.

Piker goes from discussing the Israeli government extracting sperm from deceased Israeli soldiers and civilians (a practice he said is motivated by a вҖңeugenicist, fascist, ethno-supremacist, religious supremacist mentalityвҖқ); to laughing at a clip of scholar Norman Finkelstein eviscerating Donald Trump and his administration; to reacting to Sabrina CarpenterвҖҷs confused response to a fanвҖҷs zaghrouta during her show; to joking about a fan-made TikTok about him; to analyzing US-Iran ceasefire talks. He then talks about Vice President JD VanceвҖҷs old supposed admiration for Bernie Sanders, pivots back to the ceasefire discussion, then to a Bollywood actress being attacked for simply following him, then back to shifting global interests in response to the US-Israeli war on Iran, and so on and so forth.

And in between, he keeps responding to comments, his tone oscillating constantly. Thousands of viewers are asking him questions, laughing at his jokes, offering their own forms of analysis, expressing gratitude for his thoughts, wishing each other good morning or good night, or simply opining as flippantly as Piker.

вҖңвҖҳHey man, I just got here, can you start the stream over?вҖҷвҖқ one chatter says.

вҖңSure buddy, I got you,вҖқ Piker replies, flashing the waiting room graphic on the screen, and then re-fading it off. вҖңWhatвҖҷs up everybody, IвҖҷm live and alive,вҖқ he says, with a chuckle. вҖңFucking idiot.вҖқ

Piker first began streaming in 2018, while he still worked at The Young Turks, the media company co-founded by his uncle, Cenk Uygur. His days were long. HeвҖҷd start writing a morning breakdown for TYT starting at 5am. Then film it. Then jot down production notes and collect B-roll. HeвҖҷd look at the first edit, and provide additional notes to the editing team. Then heвҖҷd reformat the whole thing as an article to publish on HuffPost. HeвҖҷd come home and make dinner. Then heвҖҷd livestream, for four, five, or six hours.

By 2019, Piker was streaming even longer, spending much less time seeing friends or family. In 2020, he made the jump and began streaming fully independent. He would stream for 8-10 hours a day, every day.

His routine grew even more intense during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic, which is also when he lost his earlier dog, Fish, to cancer. вҖңIt broke me,вҖқ Piker says. (He would not feel ready to go on to get Kaya until 2023.)

вҖңI was all alone, lost my best friend, and I guess thatвҖҷs also part of the reason why I just dove into Twitch streaming,вҖқ he continues. вҖңI livestreamed every waking moment.вҖқ He livestreamed for 42% of the entire year, he estimates. (Now, itвҖҷs around 30%, or less, he adds.)

Many people during the pandemic were naturally looking for community.

It was during that time that many of PikerвҖҷs listeners found him. To this day, the most common thing he hears from people in public is that they first discovered him in 2020.

He tells me he didnвҖҷt regret how intense his schedule felt back then, even at its most consuming, because it was worth it. ThereвҖҷs, of course, the financial independence and freedom heвҖҷs been able to build. He does have a pricey house, something critics have tried to suggest is hypocritical for someone with his politics.

But, there was something bigger than the material success. вҖңI have a massive community of like-minded people whose minds have changed throughout the years, who are very active in their own communities, and are fighting the good fight,вҖқ he says, whether they are active online, or organizing protests and rallies, and bringing more people into the fold. вҖңIвҖҷve got so many people in my community that have gotten their workplaces unionized. So I am eternally grateful for that.вҖқ

Now, PikerвҖҷs schedule has calcified into reading the news at 6am, taking Kaya to the dog park, exercising, returning home, freshening up, and streaming by around 11am.

Fitness has buoyed Piker through the demanding schedule. Accordingly, much of the public perception around him relates to his body. Article after article over the years have, ironically or not, highlighted his body, height, and physical appearance. вҖңItвҖҷs not great,вҖқ he says when I ask about how central his body seems to how he is perceived by observers. His work may be in a visual medium, but his fitness routine is for him.

вҖңI work out because it makes me feel better. I work out and want to have a healthy body because it helps me deal with everything else that IвҖҷm doing,вҖқ he says, explaining that the regimen helps him deal with the amount of streaming, and thus sitting, he does, and the gravity of the content he covers. вҖңIf I skip working out for a couple days, I start feeling like shit,вҖқ he explains. вҖңI get sad, get a little depressed, so I definitely have to keep up with that.вҖқ

Piker started working out in earnest after college, when he moved to Los Angeles. His friends were personal trainers who were very keen with their diets, spurring him to find his discipline. And soon, the day arrived: He got his own six-pack.

Piker says the milestone instilled something new heвҖҷs carried beyond fitness. вҖңIt sounds silly, but itвҖҷs one of the most consequential things that I always wanted, and I could never achieve it,вҖқ he says. вҖңAnd now that I have achieved it, I know that I have the power to achieve whatever I want to, as long as I work really hard, and IвҖҷm very stubborn, and push through all of the hurdles.вҖқ

As much as he is a political lightning rod, Piker is also an avatar for young people around the world. Especially when it comes to navigating online landmines surrounding fitness and body image.

Piker says heвҖҷs been subject to some of the same social forces perturbing young people today: pockets of the internet that push people towards eating disorders, and other online spaces that feed an вҖңunhealthy obsessionвҖқ with oneвҖҷs looks. He notes itвҖҷs become вҖңa universal experience all teenagers go through.вҖқ

Piker emphasizes that teenage girls were already dealing with these issues, but, thanks to the вҖңmanosphere,вҖқ boys now find themselves navigating similar problems.

ItвҖҷs among the topics he addresses in his commentary, to encourage young people to have a healthier outlook not grounded solely in aesthetics.

вҖңIвҖҷm on the other side of it now, so I donвҖҷt really care about it as much, but, back then, I think I was just young and insecure,вҖқ he says. вҖңI still have body dysmorphiaвҖҰ IвҖҷm aware of it. So when I see myself, I know that the way I see myself is always far worse than how others perceive me.вҖқ

At one point in our conversation, as we discuss what Piker was like as a child, PikerвҖҷs mom, who was present within earshot at the time, chimes in.

вҖңVery stylish,вҖқ she quips. She says Piker was very interested in drawing, seemingly an outlet for his energy. She brings out some of his old sketchbooks. вҖңIt wasnвҖҷt enough that he drew in his notebooks. He drew all over the desk as wellвҖҰ and he was super hyperactive,вҖқ she recalled.

We leaf through his old notebooks. Sketches of anime, comic book and video game characters, and body forms adorn the pages. As he looks back on the drawings, Piker recounts how he actually wanted to pursue art as a career.

вҖңAt school, he was a menace,вҖқ his mom says, joking: вҖңThe teachers would cry when they saw me.вҖқ

вҖңHe could question everything,вҖқ she adds. вҖңThatвҖҷs part of the problem.вҖқ

Instead of becoming an artist, Piker has built one of the largest online audiences in US politics, including the largest political audience on Twitch in the US. His audience is uniquely large for a leftist content creator. He also speaks to a younger audience. All of it makes him a threat to the political establishment, and it also means he speaks with less of a filter, more profanely, which leaves what he says more open to exploitation by his enemies.

Piker has shined a spotlight on IsraelвҖҷs US-backed genocide in Palestine, railed against the ills of Western capitalism and corporate greed, and used his large platform to spread class consciousness and support leftist political candidates.

As a result, from Fox to the Democratic Party establishment, Piker has gone from an inconvenient thorn to a massive target.

For his critics on the right, like those at Fox, PikerвҖҷs response is simple and echoes FDR: вҖңTheyвҖҷre united in their hatred of me and I welcome their hatred.вҖқ

вҖңThis is going to be corny to say, but: вҖҳTheir boos mean nothing when IвҖҷve seen what makes them cheer,вҖҷвҖқ he says. вҖңThatвҖҷs the Rick and Morty quote, I canвҖҷt believe IвҖҷm saying that, but itвҖҷs a bar and it fits so perfectly in this circumstance. IвҖҷm sorry. If you are championing Donald Trump and his Epstein buddies, the pedophilic rapist class of finance-capital overlords that have dominated American society unconditionally, youвҖҷve defended a man who ran on Haitians eating cats and dogs, engaging in white vengeance, white nativist politics, insistently dividing the working class in this country and saying a great deal of heinous things, including, but not limited to, a couple of weeks ago, saying heвҖҷs going to do a genocide, heвҖҷs going to knock off the entire Iranian вҖҳcivilizationвҖҷ overnight. This person is a monster. If youвҖҷre defending that guy, I donвҖҷt care if you turn around and call me a radical. I donвҖҷt care if you turn around and say IвҖҷm an enemy вҖ“ I am an enemy to you. You are my enemy.вҖқ

And for those in the center вҖ“ like the centrist think tank Third Way, which has demanded that Democratic politicians condemn Piker вҖ“ he shares similar sentiments, arguing that such groups вҖңare not real.вҖқ

вҖңTheyвҖҷre a group of consultants that just get paid by lifeless, faceless, parasitic capital [to] dig around and try to be a mediator, a middleman, between the interests of Exxon, Chevron, Aetna, Blue Cross Blue Shield, and Democratic Party electeds,вҖқ he says.

Recently, politicians and pundits from the far right and the center left have dredged up a litany of PikerвҖҷs most controversial comments, often lacking context, in order to try to discredit and smear him.

Perhaps the most weaponized comment is when he said that вҖңAmerica deserved 9/11.вҖқ

Piker was reacting to a 2019 clip of podcaster Joe Rogan speaking with Republican Rep. Dan Crenshaw. Rogan was asking Crenshaw whether US actions or its massive military presence around the world may foment terrorism. Crenshaw dismissed the notion. At one point вҖ“ explaining why the US maintains presence in Syria, Iraq, and Afghanistan вҖ“ he said вҖңthese people over there wake up every single day trying to plan another 9/11.вҖқ Throughout the discussion, Crenshaw suggested threats against the US were incited more by ideology, and hatred of the West, rather than US actions.

The US military presence in over 100 countries is because вҖңweвҖҷre there to partner with them,вҖқ Crenshaw claimed.

вҖңI just fucking snapped,вҖқ Piker tells me. вҖңI was like, вҖҳWhat is this fucking mentality? Like, this is exactly why, you know, Americans deserved 9/11, we totally brought it upon ourselves.вҖҷ And that part never gets вҖ“ I mean, that part is in the clips, but they donвҖҷt even understand what IвҖҷm saying at that point: We are responsible for 9/11, and we are still pursuing the same mentality even to this day. What I was referencing was blowback.вҖқ

He continues, вҖңI donвҖҷt think people that bring it up as a real problem are doing so because they think IвҖҷm pro 9/11. Who the fuck is pro-9/11?вҖқ Piker poses. вҖңTheyвҖҷre just saying that to be like, вҖҳThis is a Muslim guy who loves terrorism. We got him. He proved it himself.вҖҷвҖқ

Piker has also been made out to be an antisemite. One common quote cited by his critics is Piker describing some Orthodox Jewish people in Israel as вҖңinbred.вҖқ

He has expressed regret about the comment, but countered that heвҖҷs used the term as a pejorative against a wide array of groups, pointing to a compilation of him calling вҖңcalling every kind of reactionary, neo-Nazi, white supremacist Republicans, вҖҳinbreds.вҖҷвҖқ

вҖңItвҖҷs vulgar. I know itвҖҷs vulgar. I want to be hurtful. I want to be mean to these people that are doing heinous crimes,вҖқ Piker tells me. HeвҖҷs confounded by those who read the quote by itself, as if he was вҖңrandomly calling Jewish people inbreeders.вҖқ

вҖңThereвҖҷs a game of telephone with these quotes where it originates from me saying, вҖҳLook at these racist inbred ultra-Zionist settlers, destroying humanitarian aid at the Gaza border crossing,вҖҷ to вҖҳHasan Piker called Orthodox Jews inbred,вҖҷ to вҖҳHasan Piker calls all Jews inbred,вҖҷ and itвҖҷs not the case,вҖқ he says.

One of PikerвҖҷs fans created a 30-minute compilation of the streamer speaking against antisemitism. He has called antisemitism вҖңa canary in the coal mine of fascism.вҖқ HeвҖҷs said thereвҖҷs вҖңnothing grosser than both being antisemitic and then using the cause of Palestine to feed people more antisemitic commentary.вҖқ HeвҖҷs repeatedly argued against conflating the government of Israel with Jewish people.

A newer line of attack against Piker has been the suggestion that he is illiberal, an avowed authoritarian. Piker has been accused of peddling pro-Communist propaganda during a recent visit to China, and has been criticized for saying, at a recent event at Yale University, that the fall of the Soviet Union was вҖңone of the greatest catastrophes of the 20th century.вҖқ

For the former, Piker has acknowledged ChinaвҖҷs censorship practices, noting how he could not do what he does in the US in China. But, he said, the hope of his visit was to вҖңshow that China isnвҖҷt this hermit kingdom the way itвҖҷs often presented,вҖқ and foster вҖңa better understanding of other people.вҖқ As for the latter, Piker has argued that the fall of the USSR resulted in вҖңincalculableвҖқ harm to the nations that were part of it, and that it ended a multipolar world, accelerating neoliberalism that is вҖңdevastating every Western nation.вҖқ

Piker has not stood by all his past remarks. In an old sketch video from 2013, he joked: вҖңCome back again next week, when we discuss the legendary question of all time вҖ“ old enough to count, old enough to mount?вҖқ

вҖңIt was a failed attempt at satire that was supposed to be like a bro show, and it was so cringe and so awful that I immediately deleted it,вҖқ Piker tells me.

He frames that moment as part of his journey. He notes he mostly had progressive opinions at the time, but was transphobic and misogynistic, too. вҖңThat is something that I have talked about openly for many years at this point,вҖқ Piker says. вҖңI talk about privilege blind spots, and the intersectionality of privilege, and how we also have to be more graceful вҖ“ and I know itвҖҷs difficult, especially if youвҖҷre a part of the vulnerable group that is being offended вҖ“ but I talk about how we have to be a little bit more graceful and remind ourselves that everyone is on a journey and that we, as leftists, have to be more invested in rehabilitation.вҖқ

Piker is not just armchair quarterbacking. He has been active in supporting candidates like New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani. He recently mobilized voters at two rallies alongside progressive primary candidates such as Abdul El-Sayed, whoвҖҷs running for Senate in Michigan. HeвҖҷs platformed congressional candidates like Adam Hamawy in New Jersey вҖ“ a doctor who served in Gaza and is credited with saving Illinois Senator Tammy DuckworthвҖҷs life during the Iraq War.

After PikerвҖҷs Michigan rallies were announced, El-SayedвҖҷs opponents launched a smear campaign against him, with Michigan Senator Elissa Slotkin and candidates Haley Stevens and Mallory McMorrow loudly condemning Piker. The campaign did not work. Recent polling has shown Piker to have positive favorability ID in the state, albeit with little name recognition. And, after Piker appeared alongside El-Sayed, a poll showed El-Sayed surging in the primary.

Piker weathered similar failed attacks when he came out in support of Mamdani and had him on his show.

Outside of traditional party politics, Piker seeks to push people into organizations like the Democratic Socialists of America, where they can find an organizing community, and help show the country a different way of doing politics.

To him, MamdaniвҖҷs popularity illustrates this. вҖңAll of a sudden, people look at him and go, вҖҳWait a minute. This guy says heвҖҷs a Democratic socialist. I have learned my whole life that socialism is a scary thing, but this is a handsome guy who smiles a lot, whoвҖҷs fixing the potholes.вҖҷ ThatвҖҷs sewer socialism through and through,вҖқ he says.

Speaking more about MamdaniвҖҷs governance, Piker adds: вҖңI think that is very helpful in advancing this cause and showing Americans that there is a way to do more responsive politics, a way to reinstill confidence in good governance. Because I think many Americans, understandably, are very resistant to that mentality that your government is supposed to help you when you need it, your government is supposed to fix certain things, because it hasnвҖҷt. So thatвҖҷs what IвҖҷm trying to achieve, to let 1,000 Zohrans bloom.вҖқ

Of course, the Democratic Party establishment didnвҖҷt want the first Zohran to bloom. That has made PikerвҖҷs relationship вҖңantagonistic at times, unnecessarily,вҖқ he says, because, in his view, heвҖҷs seeking to help the party.

вҖңIf this is supposed to be a more representative force of the needs of the masses, they should act like it, but they donвҖҷt, because theyвҖҷre captured by corporate interests and that is my main issue with the Democratic Party,вҖқ he says.

Instead, to his critics, heвҖҷs an unproductive spoiler вҖ“ вҖңbecause I dare demand more,вҖқ as he puts it.

Piker did not endorse Kamala Harris in 2024 вҖ“ a decision he still stands by. He tried to warn her campaign about the political risks posed by the Biden administrationвҖҷs support for IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza, and her own campaignвҖҷs refusal to engage voters on the issue.

Beyond some sparse verbal overtures, the pleas were largely ignored. The Democratic National Convention вҖ“ where Piker streamed from вҖ“ famously refused to allow a Palestinian to speak on stage, further inflaming tensions within the party over Israel and Gaza.

Piker ultimately advised people to vote their conscience. He wasnвҖҷt going to castigate anyone for their vote, unless it was for Donald Trump.

On April 10, Piker attended a film screening in Los Angeles of вҖҳThe Voice of Hind Rajab,вҖҷ a documentary about Israel murdering a 5-year-old girl, after killing her family members in front of her.

Piker addressed an audience of 100-some attendees, and discussed how the Democratic Party could be pushed вҖңin the appropriate directionвҖқ despite some Democrats having participated вҖңenthusiastically in the genocide of the PalestiniansвҖқ up to this point.

He recalls how much he cried when he first saw the film. It was a вҖңreleaseвҖқ of the emotion he banked up from hours every day covering the thousands of kids just like Hind killed by Israeli forces. That coverage has also included interviewing a teenager live from Gaza who had been displaced three times within weeks amid Israeli bombardment, and amplifying doctors who served in Gaza, who recounted their experiences surviving Israeli violence and treating horrific injuries.

ItвҖҷs this kind of horror that Piker, and millions of his viewers, have witnessed вҖ“ and theyвҖҷve seen how many people in power have not only refused to contend with it, but actively supported it.

Today, IsraelвҖҷs violence is not just a concern among the left. It is increasingly mainstream to oppose IsraelвҖҷs genocidal violence in Palestine, and beyond, and to oppose unconditional US support for it. That is especially the case in the Democratic Party, where majorities now view Israel unfavorably.

Some on the right do, too. Two of the most prominent figures representing this shift are former far-right Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene and ex-Fox host Tucker Carlson, who agree with human rights experts and progressive leaders that Israel is committing genocide.

Their views have spurred talk of a potential left-right coalition вҖ“ a conversation that has been stoked by Greene and progressive Rep. Ro Khanna, as well as PikerвҖҷs uncle Cenk Uygur and his colleague Ana Kasparian. Piker said heвҖҷs thrown out вҖңfeelersвҖқ about going on CarlsonвҖҷs show but hasnвҖҷt heard back, and that heвҖҷd probably be willing to host Carlson on his show too.

Still, Piker finds the left-right coalition idea is вҖңtoo media-brained.вҖқ Proponents are missing the numbers, Piker says. He notes CarlsonвҖҷs approval plummeted on the right amid his public disagreements with Trump, and that Republican approval for military action in Iran shot up after Trump decided to launch his illegal war.

In any case, Piker has his eye on Carlson. вҖңI think that he is establishing himself as a credible anti-war voice right now вҖ“ and maybe itвҖҷs actually sincere, IвҖҷm even willing to entertain that. Outside of all the other heinous things that he believes, maybe this time he saw too many dead children and that changed his mind in some way on this issue,вҖқ Piker says.

Whether or not CarlsonвҖҷs intentions are genuine, Piker adds, the former Fox host could use that вҖңestablished credibility to either runвҖқ in 2028 вҖ“ or вҖңlend that credibility to a JD Vance-style figure.вҖқ

And if the Democrats run a вҖңGavin Newsom-typeвҖқ against Carlson or whomever he blesses, Piker says, вҖңThey will destroy the Democratic Party, 100%, and we will deserve it.вҖқ

вҖңOur inability to address this issue two election cycles in a row, our resistance to our own baseвҖҷs desires, it will be devastating, and it will be entirely in the hands of the Democratic Party, just as last electionвҖҷs defeat was,вҖқ he says.

As we sit in his office discussing the litany of attacks heвҖҷs weathered, Piked refers to a title bestowed on him by comedian Brennan Lee Mulligan.

Earlier this year, during a podcast discussion about PikerвҖҷs role in the media and streaming landscape, Mulligan referred to him as a вҖңsin eater for the world.вҖқ

As Piker summarizes it, Mulligan described him as someone who вҖңhas to suffer for every leftist sin and any gripe that anyone has, any animosity they feel toward like what they perceive as woke or left or whatever. I am the human embodiment of that.вҖқ

But how, in a world filled with so much horror, has Piker come to fulfill this role? For whom is Piker the embodiment of sin?

In the US, there is an untenable status quo вҖ“ one that the political and media class seeks to preserve. There is colossal and expanding wealth inequality, underscored by an increasingly visible crop of billionaires, and of sexual abusers infiltrating all facets of public life. Costs of living are prohibitive, and only getting worse thanks to the policy decisions of the people in charge вҖ“ who are far more focused on funding genocide and foreign wars than attending to the needs of the people they represent.

As public opinion shifts, particularly on Israel, elites are looking to stop the bleeding. For them, Piker represents both a convenient target, based on his history of controversial remarks, and a necessary one.

Because itвҖҷs not his body, style, vulgarity, controversy, or methods alone that have brought Piker so central to the moment. ItвҖҷs that he offers a place in which people can process the chaos all around them. He presents a mirror to the layperson вҖ“ and a window to the world вҖ“ so they can see they are not crazy. And he insists that, no matter how vulgar he is or is made to be, nothing can match the obscenity of Israel murdering a child, surrounded by the corpses of her family members, and the world answering with resounding silence.

For those whoвҖҷve helped create this world, itвҖҷs necessary that Hasan Piker eats sin. Because otherwise, he is an invitation for everyone else to understand whose sins they actually are.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: *2.45PM ET TODAY* Top Catholic Priest on the Pope vs Donald Trump and JD Vance
item.description: Jesuit priest James Martin has defended Pope Leo from TrumpвҖҷs attacks and is challenging the weaponization of Christianity to justify war in Iran. He'll take your questions during a live Town Hall. Jesuit priest James Martin has defended Pope Leo from TrumpвҖҷs attacks and is challenging theвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Jesuit priest James Martin has defended Pope Leo from TrumpвҖҷs attacks and is challenging the weaponization of Christianity to justify war in Iran. He'll take your questions during a live Town Hall.

Jesuit priest James Martin has defended Pope Leo from TrumpвҖҷs attacks and is challenging the weaponization of Christianity to justify war in Iran. He'll take your questions during a live Town Hall.

Photo illustration showing a picture of Donald Trump and an AI-generated image he posted online depicting himself as Jesus Christ. Photo by Mandel NGAN/AFP via Getty Images

President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance and other members of the administration have been engaged in a strange battle with Pope Leo XIV, sparked by the PontiffвҖҷs stance against the war in Iran. ItвҖҷs just the latest in a long streak of disrespect towards Christians and Christianity throughout TrumpвҖҷs presidency. As administration officials defend the US strikes in Iran as a вҖңjust war,вҖқ Catholic leaders are rejecting this claim, saying that it does not fit the doctrine of just war established by the Church.

Rev. James Martin, a Jesuit priest, best-selling author on Catholicism, and editor at large of America Magazine, has warned about efforts by American politicians to вҖңmarginalizeвҖқ the PopeвҖҷs voice and convince people to not listen to the Holy FatherвҖҷs words denouncing warmongers.

Martin will join Mehdi in a live Zeteo town hall today, Tuesday, at 2:45pm ET (11:45am PT / 7:45pm GMT) to discuss the Trump administrationвҖҷs attacks on the Pope, its weaponization of Christian beliefs, and whether there is any truth to the claims of a вҖңjust war.вҖқ

TheyвҖҷll also discuss that now-notorious and deleted image that Trump posted portraying himself as Jesus Christ.

Attendees will be limited to paid Zeteo subscribers, who have the chance to ask their own questions to Martin as well. Register now below.

item.title: Trump Is Making Republicans Wet Their Beds
item.description: On this day in 1966, three activists staged a вҖңSip-InвҖқ in Greenwich Village in New York, demanding service from bars refusing to serve gay men. Predating the more famous Stonewall Riot by three years, the protest was nonetheless a key event in the movement for gay rights. Good morning. ItвҖҷs SwinвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1966, three activists staged a вҖңSip-InвҖқ in Greenwich Village in New York, demanding service from bars refusing to serve gay men. Predating the more famous Stonewall Riot by three years, the protest was nonetheless a key event in the movement for gay rights.

Good morning. ItвҖҷs Swin again. Today is another day ending in вҖңY,вҖқ so that means itвҖҷs another day of President Donald Trumptrying to rig the midterms. You should not be complacent at all about this authoritarian threat, but his corrupt efforts are probably going to fail. One of the key reasons for that is that this president is so towering in his unpopularity that it makes the 2026 elections even harder to steal than they otherwise would have been. ItвҖҷs an acidic level of unpopularity that TrumpвҖҷs party and administration reeeeealllllllly cannot spin away вҖ“ and boy, is there panic starting to show in some darkly comic and desperate ways. More on that belowвҖҰ

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ Republicans know their policies are a political problem, TrumpвҖҷs scandal-plagued labor secretary bites the dust, GOP lawmakers have a bill to denaturalize US citizens, and hopefully, soon youвҖҷll have a good reason to read Infowars.

In the past few months, according to three sources with direct knowledge of this matter, officials in the Trump White House have privately begged a number of their hardcore, anti-immigration allies to lay off the president for the rest of the year. The White HouseвҖҷs secret message goes as follows: Please stop yelling at us for pausing certain tactics since the height of our Minnesota siege. Republicans need to win the midterms. Our mass deportation policies are clearly a drag on our poll numbers. We promise to ramp the ethnic cleansing back up to 11 after the midterms, okay?

I am, of course, paraphrasing. But the message was loud and clear: GOP leaders have seen all the numbers, and are queasy about how their own policy priorities might be doing them in, electorally speaking. вҖңIвҖҷve heard that argument a million times, and itвҖҷs generally accepted as the political posture of this administration вҖ“ and itвҖҷs widely rejected by those of us in the Mass Deportation Coalition,вҖқ Mike Howell, a former Trump DHS official who is leading a new network dedicated to pressuring the Trump-Vance administration on this issue, tells me. вҖңWe were told not to talk about mass deportation before the midterms, so instead we are screaming it вҖ“ across the country.вҖқ

To be fair to HowellвҖҷs coalition, they are only asking President Trump to keep doing what he promised the country, if not world, that he would do in a second term. But itвҖҷs not just TrumpвҖҷs militarized ethnic cleansing thatвҖҷs giving conservative politicians and consultants reason to fear the wrath of the midterms.

Ahead of Election Day in November, you could easily argue that the Republican PartyвҖҷs greatest obstacle to keep the House and the Senate isnвҖҷt the woefully inept Democratic Party. ItвҖҷs TrumpвҖҷs (and the Republican PartyвҖҷs) disastrous, illegal bloodbath in the Middle East, which is jacking up gas prices and shoving the United States вҖ“ and planet вҖ“ to the brink of complete economic rupture.

Since last year, weвҖҷve been reporting on how behind the scenes, the national Republican elite have been freaking out that TrumpвҖҷs stagnantly вҖң awfulвҖқ poll numbers would invariably drag his party down in the 2026 elections. As of this month вҖ“ per conversations IвҖҷve had with an array of Trump appointees, longtime Republican operatives, conservative media luminaries, and GOP lawmakers вҖ“ those anxieties have only skyrocketed at a higher altitude, and itвҖҷs in large part driven by the Iran war. They all say theyвҖҷre fucked. TheyвҖҷre just trying to figure out just how badly. And yet, primarily out of fear of

вҖңThe bedwetting is making the whole bedroom damp,вҖқ a senior Trump administration official, speaking on the condition of anonymity (naturally), tells me.

They add that the intra-party freakout is now worse than what you see in public view, even. And boy, is what we see publicly really badвҖҰ if youвҖҷre a Republican.

With the presidentвҖҷs approval rating sliding deeper into the abyss, the Trump White House convened an emergency meeting at a luxury DC hotel with top GOP operatives in an effort to figure out how to staunch the bleeding. Fresh polling has come out showing that the majority of registered voters (correctly) blame the Republican president for spiking gas prices. GOP politicians and operatives in battleground states keep openly warning that the Senate is slipping out of their hands вҖ“ something that wasnвҖҷt a pervasive fear all that many months ago. And top Republican senators canвҖҷt seem to stop begging this president вҖ“ with tears in their eyes, as he would say вҖ“ to pretty-please end his war, so that they donвҖҷt get electorally slaughtered come November.

вҖңI hope that we are arriving at an exit strategy here to bring this to a close to preserve our security interests and bring down the cost of gasoline,вҖқ Senator Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) told the press in recent days, adding that the вҖң clock is ticking,вҖқ and speaking with all the confidence of a hostage under a searingly annoying level of duress.

In their hopes of forestalling a historic midterms implosion, the GOP elite is praying (sometimes literally) for cheaper gas. On Sunday, TrumpвҖҷs energy secretary, Chris Wright, went on TV to say that Americans may have to expect gas to remain above $3 per gallon into next year.

In response to hearing about this, one of the GOPвҖҷs most prominent national operatives only had this to message me: вҖңHOLY SHIT JUST SAY YOU LIKE LOSING.вҖқ

So things are going great.

Death tolls:

Iran: An Iranian official said this week that the death toll from US-Israeli strikes is at 3,375, including 383 children. It was unclear whether the death toll also included military and security forces.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,294 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to figures from the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 34 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war newsвҖҰ

Screed test: In a rant, Trump called Democratic critics of his war вҖңweak and patheticвҖқ and вҖңtraitors all,вҖқ and insisted the Iran war was вҖңon the scale of VenezuelaвҖқ but at the same time вҖңjust a bigger, more complex operation.вҖқ

Resume fire: Trump signaled that he will likely not extend the ceasefire set to expire on Wednesday, and threatened to drop bombs if no deal is reached. The president said he expected peace talks to continue, but said he was in no rush to end the conflict.

Threats threat: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf said Tehran would refuse to engage in talks with the US if Trump continued making threats.

Force majeure: Kuwait said it could not fulfill obligations to deliver crude oil and refined products thanks to circumstances in the Strait of Hormuz.

Dire warnings: UN officials are sounding the alarm on the energy crisis due to the war in Iran. The conflict has triggered вҖңthe most severe energy crisis in a generation,вҖқ said Secretary-General AntГіnio Guterres. Meanwhile, UN climate chief Simon Stiell said: вҖңFossil fuel-driven stagflation is now stalking economies, driving up prices, driving down growth, pushing budgets deeper into the quagmire of debt and stripping away governmentsвҖҷ policy options and autonomy.вҖқ

Lebanon latest: Lebanese media reported that Israel was continuing its strikes on villages along the border, despite the 10-day ceasefire in place. Meanwhile, Spain, Slovenia, and Ireland urged the European Union to debate suspending its association agreement with Israel over its actions in Gaza, the West Bank, and Lebanon.

For months, IвҖҷve wanted to write a piece for Zeteo explaining how world-historically wild it is that the Trump-Vance administration is so corrupt, so violently authoritarian, so scandal-plagued that you simply did not know what is going on at the Department of Labor right now. (Ok, maybe some of you did.) But President Trump wonвҖҷt stop invading and bombing places, so IвҖҷve been busy and never wrote it. So IвҖҷll make the point here.

On Monday, NOTUS broke the news that TrumpвҖҷs labor secretary, Lori Chavez-DeRemer, had finally resigned.

It was about damn time. During any other administration in modern US history, the things that occurred at the highest levels of her Labor Department would have dominated cable news. The tumult at the Scott Pruitt-era EPA certainly drove TV news cycles during Trump 1.0, and yet all of that now looks downright quaint compared to the depraved-reality-TV-style flood of scandal and alleged misconduct that came out of Chavez-DeRemerвҖҷs Labor Department. HereвҖҷs a sample of headlines from recent months:

People: вҖңLabor SecretaryвҖҷs Husband and Father Sent Personal Texts to Her Young Female Staffers, Report Claims: вҖҳPlease Keep This PrivateвҖҷвҖқ

NYT: вҖңLabor SecretaryвҖҷs Husband Barred From the Department After Sexual Assault ReportsвҖқ

CBS: вҖңLabor secretaryвҖҷs husband wonвҖҷt face charges after being banned from Labor Dept. building, sources sayвҖқ

Politico: вҖңDC police searched Labor SecretaryвҖҷs office in sexual assault investigationвҖқ

New York Post: вҖңLabor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemerвҖҷs security guard resigns amid probe into allegations of вҖҳinappropriateвҖҷ relationship: sourcesвҖқ

Independent: вҖңTrumpвҖҷs labor secretary accused of taking staff to a strip club on official trip, report saysвҖқ

Salem Statesman Journal: вҖңLori Chavez-DeRemer reportedly under investigation for misusing fundsвҖқ

Chavez-DeRemerвҖҷs story was buried under the full tonnage of media coverage devoted to the numerous other scandals, abuses, wars, killing sprees, cover-ups, rantings and ravings, and eruptions of all-American fascism that the second Trump administration produces on an hourly basis.

After Chavez-DeRemer resigned Monday, White House communications director Steven Cheung said she did вҖңa phenomenal job.вҖқ

Keep in mind: Chavez-DeRemer was supposed to be the normal one when Trump was putting together his cabinet of debauched clowns and madmen. Clearly, she was never an outlier.

Suits you: FBI Director Kash Patel filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic for its report about allegations of excessive drinking, erratic behavior, and absences from work, which Patel denies. The Atlantic plans to fight the suit.

Class Onion: The Onion announced its takeover of Infowars, long run by on-off Trump ally and Sandy Hook denier Alex Jones. The satirical title aims to relaunch the conspiracist website as a parody of itself.

Union dues: In a blow for centrist Democrats, the Colorado AFL-CIO endorsed Julie Gonzales, a progressive challenger to sitting US senator John Hickenlooper.

Acting up: Far-right Texas GOP congressman Chip Roy introduced the MAMDANI Act, an Islamophobic measure named after the New York mayor and containing вҖңMeasures Against MarxismвҖҷs Dangerous Adherents and Noxious Islamists,вҖқ including deportation and denaturalization.

Cleaning House: After the exits of Democrat Eric Swalwell and Republican Tony Gonzales, the House Ethics Committee said it would maintain a вҖңworkplace free fromвҖқ sexual misconduct. Republican Cory Mills still faces such allegations. Nancy Mace filed a motion to expel, calling for вҖңmoral courage to do whatвҖҷs right.вҖқ

Profit alert: The far-right influencer and Holocaust denier Nick Fuentes has made $900,000 from donors since last year, the Washington Post reported, detailing giving by people with little money of their own, including through paid messages or вҖңsuperchats.вҖқ

For which old-time practice did Texas Republican Chip Roy appear to advocate in a 2021 US House hearing about anti-Asian hate, earning widespread rebuke?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

Jesuit priest James Martin will join Mehdi in a live Zeteo town hall TODAY at 2:45pm ET (11:45am PT / 7:45pm GMT) to discuss the Trump administrationвҖҷs attacks on the pope, its weaponization of Christian beliefs, the blasphemous AI image of the president as Jesus, and whether there is any truth to the claims of a вҖңjust warвҖқ against Iran.

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: The EU, UN, and World Bank estimated that reconstruction and recovery will require $71.4 billion over a decade.

рҹҮөрҹҮё Deadly day in West Bank: Israeli settlers killed two Palestinians, including a 13-year-old boy, and injured several others during an attack on a school in the village of al-Mughayyir, east of Ramallah, Palestinian media reported. Separately, a car tied to an Israeli ministerвҖҷs security detail hit and killed a 16-year-old Palestinian on a bike, Haaretz reported.

рҹҮ°рҹҮј Reporter held: Today marks 50 days in detention in Kuwait for Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, arrested after sharing previously published pictures of a crashed US plane. Calls for his release continue.

рҹҮёрҹҮ© Switching sides: Maj. Gen. al-Nour Ahmed Adam, a top Rapid Support Forces (RSF) commander, left the group to join SudanвҖҷs national army, four years after the civil war began, the Sudan Tribune said.

рҹҮәрҹҮ¬рҹҮЁрҹҮ¬ Captives out: Ugandan and Congolese forces freed more than 200 civilians from Allied Democratic Forces in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.


The GOP is the party of xenophobia and white supremacy. And theyвҖҷre not even ashamed of it. This racist, anti-Black congressman, by the way, is married to the daughter ofвҖҰ Indian immigrants.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

CBS: вҖңLabor secretaryвҖҷs husband wonвҖҷt face charges after being banned from Labor Dept. building, sources sayвҖқ

Politico: вҖңDC police searched Labor SecretaryвҖҷs office in sexual assault investigationвҖқ

New York Post: вҖңLabor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemerвҖҷs security guard resigns amid probe into allegations of вҖҳinappropriateвҖҷ relationship: sourcesвҖқ

Independent: вҖңTrumpвҖҷs labor secretary accused of taking staff to a strip club on official trip, report saysвҖқ

Salem Statesman Journal: вҖңLori Chavez-DeRemer reportedly under investigation for misusing fundsвҖқ

Chavez-DeRemerвҖҷs story was buried under the full tonnage of media coverage devoted to the numerous other scandals, abuses, wars, killing sprees, cover-ups, rantings and ravings, and eruptions of all-American fascism that the second Trump administration produces on an hourly basis.

After Chavez-DeRemer resigned Monday, White House communications director Steven Cheung said she did вҖңa phenomenal job.вҖқ

Keep in mind: Chavez-DeRemer was supposed to be the normal one when Trump was putting together his cabinet of debauched clowns and madmen. Clearly, she was never an outlier.

Suits you: FBI Director Kash Patel filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic for its report about allegations of excessive drinking, erratic behavior, and absences from work, which Patel denies. The Atlantic plans to fight the suit.

Class Onion: The Onion announced its takeover of Infowars, long run by on-off Trump ally and Sandy Hook denier Alex Jones. The satirical title aims to relaunch the conspiracist website as a parody of itself.

Union dues: In a blow for centrist Democrats, the Colorado AFL-CIO endorsed Julie Gonzales, a progressive challenger to sitting US senator John Hickenlooper.

Acting up: Far-right Texas GOP congressman Chip Roy introduced the MAMDANI Act, an Islamophobic measure named after the New York mayor and containing вҖңMeasures Against MarxismвҖҷs Dangerous Adherents and Noxious Islamists,вҖқ including deportation and denaturalization.

Cleaning House: After the exits of Democrat Eric Swalwell and Republican Tony Gonzales, the House Ethics Committee said it would maintain a вҖңworkplace free fromвҖқ sexual misconduct. Republican Cory Mills still faces such allegations. Nancy Mace filed a motion to expel, calling for вҖңmoral courage to do whatвҖҷs right.вҖқ

Profit alert: The far-right influencer and Holocaust denier Nick Fuentes has made $900,000 from donors since last year, the Washington Post reported, detailing giving by people with little money of their own, including through paid messages or вҖңsuperchats.вҖқ

For which old-time practice did Texas Republican Chip Roy appear to advocate in a 2021 US House hearing about anti-Asian hate, earning widespread rebuke?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

Jesuit priest James Martin will join Mehdi in a live Zeteo town hall TODAY at 2:45pm ET (11:45am PT / 7:45pm GMT) to discuss the Trump administrationвҖҷs attacks on the pope, its weaponization of Christian beliefs, the blasphemous AI image of the president as Jesus, and whether there is any truth to the claims of a вҖңjust warвҖқ against Iran.

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: The EU, UN, and World Bank estimated that reconstruction and recovery will require $71.4 billion over a decade.

рҹҮөрҹҮё Deadly day in West Bank: Israeli settlers killed two Palestinians, including a 13-year-old boy, and injured several others during an attack on a school in the village of al-Mughayyir, east of Ramallah, Palestinian media reported. Separately, a car tied to an Israeli ministerвҖҷs security detail hit and killed a 16-year-old Palestinian on a bike, Haaretz reported.

рҹҮ°рҹҮј Reporter held: Today marks 50 days in detention in Kuwait for Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, arrested after sharing previously published pictures of a crashed US plane. Calls for his release continue.

рҹҮёрҹҮ© Switching sides: Maj. Gen. al-Nour Ahmed Adam, a top Rapid Support Forces (RSF) commander, left the group to join SudanвҖҷs national army, four years after the civil war began, the Sudan Tribune said.

рҹҮәрҹҮ¬рҹҮЁрҹҮ¬ Captives out: Ugandan and Congolese forces freed more than 200 civilians from Allied Democratic Forces in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.


The GOP is the party of xenophobia and white supremacy. And theyвҖҷre not even ashamed of it. This racist, anti-Black congressman, by the way, is married to the daughter ofвҖҰ Indian immigrants.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

item.title: Big Tech Is Powering Israel's AI War Machine
item.description: IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza exposed how AI is changing warfare in terrifying and deadly ways. With the help of Big Tech, Israel, along with the US, is now expanding its use of AI in Iran and Lebanon, widening its testing ground for AI-enabled military technology вҖ“ with more deadly consequences.вҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza exposed how AI is changing warfare in terrifying and deadly ways. With the help of Big Tech, Israel, along with the US, is now expanding its use of AI in Iran and Lebanon, widening its testing ground for AI-enabled military technology вҖ“ with more deadly consequences.

Israel has long been one of the worldвҖҷs leading hubs for AI development. As of 2024, there were about 2,300 AI startups active in Israel, according to a report by Google Israel and RISE Israel, a technology think tank. Since then, that number has likely only risen as venture capitalists pour money into the industry. Roughly one quarter of all technology startups in Israel are AI companies, supported by billions of dollars in funding.

Tech giants have also deepened their relationships with Israel, tapping into their tech ecosystem to recruit talent and grow their businesses. Nvidia recently announced plans to build a massive tech campus in northern Israel that will be home to AI research and development projects, and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has described Israel as the companyвҖҷs вҖңsecond home.вҖқ

All of this has happened as Israel assaults civilian populations in Gaza, the West Bank, Lebanon, and Iran.

A new report from the International Institute for Strategic Studies (ISS), a think tank and leading authority on global security and military conflict, details exactly how heavily integrated AI is in IsraelвҖҷs weapons systems and military operations.

According to the report, Israel has made significant investments to embed AI across its military tools and systems. These efforts range from the creation of an AI and Autonomy Administration within the Ministry of DefenseвҖҷs Directorate of Defense Research & Development, to empowering the elite intelligence Unit 8200 to build out the Israeli militaryвҖҷs own AI capabilities.

AI is being used to improve target tracking and kill rates of anyone Israel deems to be enemy combatants. Israeli company Smart Shooter, for example, has developed an AI-powered вҖңsmartвҖқ weapon sight that can be installed on almost any firearm. Once appended to the gun, it can transform a standard rifle into a far more deadly precision-guided firearm.

Thanks to AI technology, a shooter can peer at a target using Smart ShooterвҖҷs SMASH optic system, and the software will lock onto the target and automatically track the person as they move. When the shooter goes to pull the trigger, the system automatically synchronizes the shot release as soon as the weapon is aimed properly at the target. Basically, the trigger is electronically blocked until the AI system calculates that the shot will actually hit, making each shot far more likely to be deadly. Smart ShooterвҖҷs SMASH system can also be used on drones.

One of IsraelвҖҷs most depraved AI products is known as Lavender. Throughout the genocide in Gaza, Israel has leveraged Lavender, an AI tool that aggregates large volumes of data on people and applies pattern-recognition algorithms to identify which people to kill, often tracking them and killing them at their homes along with their families, leading to maximum casualties.

Using this system, the Israeli army has marked tens of thousands of Palestinians as potential targets for assassination, according to +972 Magazine, and its AI targeting system has almost no human oversight and seems to be extremely permissive when it comes to casualties.

вҖңAccording to six Israeli intelligence officers, who have all served in the army during the current war on the Gaza Strip and had first-hand involvement with the use of AI to generate targets for assassination, Lavender has played a central role in the unprecedented bombing of Palestinians, especially during the early stages of the war,вҖқ +972 reported. вҖңIn fact, according to the sources, its influence on the militaryвҖҷs operations was such that they essentially treated the outputs of the AI machine вҖңas if it were a human decision.вҖқ

The ISS report also highlights IsraelвҖҷs use of AI facial recognition systems. Israel has rolled out sprawling facial-recognition programs in both Gaza and the West Bank. In the West Bank, the Israeli military uses systems that access a database called Wolf Pack. The database stores highly sensitive personal information on Palestinians, which is used to create intelligence profiles that are then shared with IsraelвҖҷs internal security agency, Shin Bet.

PalestinianвҖҷs biometric data is harvested at checkpoints throughout Israel, and Israeli soldiers have Wolf Pack-affiliated software installed on their smartphones in order to feed more Palestinians into their systems. Running facial-recognition programs on people without their consent on such a massive scale is strictly against international human rights laws. The right to privacy, including against facial recognition systems, is guaranteed under Article 17 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR).

None of these AI surveillance or AI weapons systems could function without support from the commercial market. American defense tech and AI companies like Palantir profit directly from their contracts with the Israeli military. In 2024, Israel and Palantir signed a strategic partnership to вҖңharness PalantirвҖҷs advanced technology in support of war-related missions.вҖқ

But also, вҖңbig-tech companies such as Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI have provided AI products to a variety of defence ministries, including the US and Israel,вҖқ the ISS report notes. вҖңA 2024 draft contract between Google and the Israeli defence ministry highlighted the latterвҖҷs pre-existing exclusive вҖҳlanding zoneвҖҷ to access cloud infrastructure, and new plans to create specific landing zones for military units.вҖқ

The report also reveals that under the terms of Project Nimbus, IsraelвҖҷs state-owned Israel Aerospace Industries and Rafael Defense Industries are required to use Google and AmazonвҖҷs cloud services for all cloud-computing needs. вҖңAs Israel stands accused of war crimes, crimes against humanity and genocide at both the International Criminal Court and the International Court of Justice, its deepening cooperation with commercial providers could potentially expose them to liability under both domestic and international-law frameworks,вҖқ ISS adds.

While AmericaвҖҷs two largest AI giants, Anthropic and OpenAI, havenвҖҷt formally partnered with the Israeli military directly, theyвҖҷre already deeply intertwined with its military actions. The Israeli military has had access to OpenAIвҖҷs GPT-4 language model via its partnership with Microsoft since August 2023. The LLM is capable of helping the military analyze billions of pieces of information and synthesize data. (Last year, Microsoft reportedly revoked IsraelвҖҷs elite intel Unit 8200 from accessing the technology, but it continues to work with other units of the Israeli military, per +972.)

While OpenAIвҖҷs terms initially banned the use of its services for вҖңmilitary and warfareвҖқ activities, the company quietly removed this clause and expanded its partnership with the US military in January 2024, amidst the escalating assault on Gaza.

Anthropic has sought to restrict use of its models by the US military in certain ways; however, Claude, AnthropicвҖҷs primary model, is embedded in PalantirвҖҷs systems and was reportedly used by the US military to identify targets in Iran. The ISS report notes that вҖңAI-enabled military technologies can be expected to continue to proliferate across Middle Eastern battlefields, scaling up the damage done to civilians and civilian objects and exacerbating humanitarian crises.вҖқ

As Big Tech companies deepen their ties with the US military and continue doing business with the Israeli military, the Middle East will remain a laboratory for AI-enabled warfare, and the innocent civilians in Gaza, the West Bank, Lebanon, and Iran will pay the price for Silicon ValleyвҖҷs latest AI experiments.

Taylor Lorenz, author of the Zeteo column, вҖҳNetwork Effect,вҖҷ is an acclaimed tech and online culture journalist. For more of TaylorвҖҷs writing, subscribe to her Substack, User Mag.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: вҖҳBlame the British EmpireвҖҷ: Mehdi and Prem on Iran, Palestine, and Kashmir
item.description: As US-Israeli violence escalates across Iran, Lebanon, and Palestine, the regionвҖҷs future becomes increasingly uncertain. In Palestine, the question remains: whatвҖҷs the path forward? Will it look like South Africa? India-Pakistan? вҖңThe British government in 1947 and 1948 created the mess in KashmirвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
As US-Israeli violence escalates across Iran, Lebanon, and Palestine, the regionвҖҷs future becomes increasingly uncertain. In Palestine, the question remains: whatвҖҷs the path forward? Will it look like South Africa? India-Pakistan?

вҖңThe British government in 1947 and 1948 created the mess in Kashmir with India-Pakistan, created the mess with Palestine with the mandateвҖҰ A lot to answer for, my home country,вҖқ says Mehdi as he answers a subscriberвҖҷs question on вҖҳAsk the Editor.вҖҷ

вҖҳAsk the EditorвҖҷ is ZeteoвҖҷs weekly Q&A show where subscribers can tune in live on Zeteo.com and YouTube. Watch the full video above to hear Prem and Mehdi also unpack the following:

Is the ceasefire in Iran breaking down?

Who is actually qualified to negotiate with Iran? JD Vance? Marco Rubio?

The words the mainstream media uses to вҖңsane-washвҖқ Donald Trump

How the pro-Israel lobby became a вҖңglobal phenomenonвҖқ

Why do Democrats always вҖңhave just enough votes to loseвҖқ key progressive votes in Congress?

вҖҰand, as Mehdi neatly puts it: вҖңWhy do we live in the dumbest timeline in the world where the guy from вҖҳHome Alone 2вҖҷ has mounted a possibly armageddon-starting war that didnвҖҷt need to be fought on behalf of an indicted war criminal from Tel Aviv, and now they donвҖҷt know how to end the war because theyвҖҷre too stupid, theyвҖҷre too dumb, theyвҖҷre too stubborn, theyвҖҷre too violent?вҖқ

Let us know what you think in the comments, and subscribe to catch our next episode of Ask the Editor every Monday at 11am ET on Zeteo.com, YouTube, or on the Substack app.

If you believe in the work we do and want to see more of it, do consider becoming a paid subscriber. A donation to Zeteo also goes a long way.

item.title: The вҖҳPeace PresidentвҖҷ Threatens an Apocalypse on Iran. AGAIN
item.description: On this day in 1979, President Jimmy Carter was photographed fending off an angry rabbit while fishing from a boat on a Georgia pond. The story was widely seen to have contributed to CarterвҖҷs defeat by Ronald Reagan in the 1980 election. Hello, readers вҖ“ itвҖҷs Swin again. Some of you have no doubtвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1979, President Jimmy Carter was photographed fending off an angry rabbit while fishing from a boat on a Georgia pond. The story was widely seen to have contributed to CarterвҖҷs defeat by Ronald Reagan in the 1980 election.

Hello, readers вҖ“ itвҖҷs Swin again. Some of you have no doubt grown accustomed to my incessant вҖҳSimpsonsвҖҷ references in this newsletter, seeing as how quoting clips from that show is one of the few ways I know how to adequately convey the sheer stupidity of the Trump-Vance era. This morning, I have another for you: President Donald TrumpвҖҷs negotiation skills during this phase of his illegal war with Iran have been on par with the pre-hockey game deal Chief Wiggum strikes with the criminals at SpringfieldвҖҷs jail. But as this past weekend showed, on top of the self-defeating tactics and bumbling own goals, you also get this presidentвҖҷs mentally desiccated eruptions of insatiable bloodlust.

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ Trump issues more genocidal threats while oil prices rise and his approval rating craters, and, in Michigan, a centrist Democrat backed by AIPAC and eyeing a seat in the US Senate finds support in short supply.

Nearly two weeks ago, Mehdi and I were on a Zeteo livestream, offering our live reactions to Donald TrumpвҖҷs just announced decision to back off from his own deadline for his own war, for which the self-professed вҖңPEACE PRESIDENTвҖқ had vowed an explicitly genocidal, вҖңcivilizationвҖқ-destroying blitz of war crimes on the Iranian people, if they didnвҖҷt give what he demanded. Trump, thankfully, blinked.

A hyper-tenuous two-week ceasefire (if you can call it that while keeping a straight face) would emerge. Still, on that livestream, I issued a warning to our viewers: That same night, Trump administration officials, citing recent conversations with the president, were stressing to us in real time that the chances that Trump would escalate the war anyways were still extremely high. At the end of the stream, Mehdi asked the panel if weвҖҷd be back in two weeks, talking about how everything went to shit. I wagered less than two weeks, hoping IвҖҷd be wrong.

ItвҖҷs been less than two weeks, and weвҖҷre coming off of a weekend where the former host of NBCвҖҷs вҖҳCelebrity ApprenticeвҖҷ somehow turned president of the United States is not only back to his schtick of casually threatening an apocalyptic barrage of infrastructure-destroying attacks and mass murder, but heвҖҷs once again sharply escalating his war of aggression in the Middle East.

вҖңMy Representatives are going to Islamabad, Pakistan вҖ“ They will be there tomorrow evening, for Negotiations вҖҰ WeвҖҷre offering a very fair and reasonable DEAL, and I hope they take it because, if they donвҖҷt, the United States is going to knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran. NO MORE MR. NICE GUY,вҖқ Trump posted Sunday morning.

Later, he announced that US armed forces had, in yet another dramatic escalation, attacked and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz, as the Americans and Iranians continued their dueling blockades. IranвҖҷs government has effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz since the start of the war, choking off the flow of oil and sending energy prices soaring, throttling the global economy.

Two senior US officials and two Trump advisers I spoke to this weekend all expected the war to significantly intensify very soon.

As he continues threatening textbook examples of blatant war crimes, Trump is at least publicly claiming that heвҖҷs optimistic about the latest round of supposed peace talks in Pakistan. Unsurprisingly, the Iranians signaled this week that they very much are not and that they consider these peace talks to be a farce. An Iranian official said earlier today that while no final decision had been made, Tehran so far had no plans to attend any upcoming talks in Islamabad.

At this time, all sides of this conflict look like theyвҖҷre slamming clenched fists on the Escalation Button вҖ“ with President Trump inviting even more economic destruction and political suicide. The world desperately needs the Strait of Hormuz open; in recent days, the price of oil shot down on the global market (though not at the US pump yet) because people were expecting (hoping?) this war would wrap up soon. Within the coming days, oil prices will likely spike again, along with the killing, and the Iranian regime is once again threatening to impose greater pain should Trump not officially cry uncle.

Technically, Trump had a way out of thisвҖҰ instead, the war could keep spiraling out of control, in an election year, to an extent where $4 gas might not seem that bad!

Whatever happens in the near term, we as a nation are at a juncture in modern history where it is commonplace, if not wholly expected, for our genocidal president вҖ“ in our name, funded by our hard-earned tax money вҖ“ to dangle horrific, вҖңcivilizationвҖқ-ending war crimes on civilian targets any time he feels like itвҖҰ and for our GOP-controlled Congress to just let him do it whenever he wants.

This is a type of uniquely pathetic barbarism that we as Americans want to believe we only read about, in chapters of world history like the Albigensian Crusade. And yet itвҖҷs routinely coming from the words and actions of our so-called leader of the free world.

We havenвҖҷt even begun to feel the long-term impacts of what Trump and his enablers and allies have done. We will continue reporting on this aggressively here at Zeteo and especially вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ in the coming days and weeks. Things are relentlessly bleak, but, heyвҖҰ at least we donвҖҷt have to worry about Donald Trump invading Cuba soon, right?

Death tolls:

Iran: An Iranian official said today that the death toll from US-Israeli strikes is at 3,375, including 383 children. It was unclear whether the death toll also included military and security forces.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,294 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to figures from the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 34 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war newsвҖҰ

Ship seized: The US said it hit and seized an Iranian cargo ship as part of its blockade of Iranian ports, prompting Tehran to promise retaliation, two days shy of the scheduled end of the ceasefire.

Trump gloats: In a post, the US president said the ship had been sanctioned since 2019 and added that US Marines were вҖңseeing whatвҖҷs on board.вҖқ

Barreling on: Oil prices rose, heading back towards $100 a barrel, after news of renewed tensions over the Strait of Hormuz.

Talks fear: European countries worry that US negotiators only care about a quick win for Trump, Reuters reported, adding that diplomats think the American rush to secure a deal with Iran will only guarantee years of complex talks to come.

Christ statue smashed: The Israeli military said a picture of one of its soldiers destroying a statue of Christ in southern Lebanon was real, adding that it вҖңviews the incident with great severityвҖқ and will take вҖңappropriate measuresвҖқ against those involved.

Donald Trump has reached new depths of unpopularity, according to an NBC News poll, with just 37% of Americans approving of his second-term performance and 50% of those who disapproved saying they did so strongly. A full two-thirds of respondents said they disapproved of TrumpвҖҷs handling of inflation and the conflict with Iran. One area where those two issues meet is at the gas pump: Two-thirds of Americans answering the survey said gas prices were a problem for them and their family. Trump was even down four points with Republicans, though 83% still backed him.

According to Politico, GOP unease is spreading to operatives in battleground states, who are beginning to voice real concern that TrumpвҖҷs unpopularity could see the Senate, and not just the House, fall to the Democrats in November.

Too true: US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz made an apparent Freudian slip, referring to TrumpвҖҷs attack on Iran, Operation Epic Fury, as вҖңOperation Economic Fury.вҖқ

Boos news: Michigan US Senate hopeful and AIPAC favorite Representative Haley Stevens was loudly booed as she spoke at the state Democratic convention.

Screens test: The Strokes ended their set at Coachella by displaying protest messages highlighting US-backed coups, killings, and interventions, including the war in Iran and support for IsraelвҖҷs attacks on Gaza.

Kash dash: FBI director Kash Patel said yesterday that a lawsuit against The Atlantic over its report about his alleged excessive drinking and unexplained absences was вҖңcoming tomorrow.вҖқ The Atlantic editor Jeffrey Goldberg said he stood by the story.

ICE watch: A federal judge in Long Island described вҖңpolice stateвҖқ tactics employed by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), including arresting people and drawing up warrants after the fact.

Court scoop: The New York Times published secret memos that showed how the Supreme Court in 2016 created the вҖңshadow docket,вҖқ the controversial mechanism that has created rulings in favor of the Trump administration.

Another progressive wins: Defeating the incumbent, Amir Makled, a lawyer who represented University of Michigan student protesters, won the Democratic nomination for the schoolвҖҷs governing board during the partyвҖҷs state endorsement convention this weekend.

Which Premier League football team does FBI director Kash Patel appear to support, judging by his controversial neckwear choices for visits to Congress?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

Is the US violating the вҖҳceasefireвҖҷ with Iran?

Join Mehdi and Prem TODAY at 11am ET, 8am PT, or 4pm GMT, for ZeteoвҖҷs вҖҳAsk the Editor.вҖҷ

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Hamas officials say theyвҖҷre willing to turn over some weapons to the Palestinian administrative committee, according to the New York Times. Meanwhile, Haaretz reported that Palestinians in Gaza say theyвҖҷre having to stand in increasingly long lines to wait for bread as flour and fuel shortages worsen amid IsraelвҖҷs continued restrictions on aid entering the Strip.

рҹҮ¬рҹҮ§ рҹҮөрҹҮё Spying on students: Al Jazeera reported today that 12 universities in the UK paid hundreds of thousands of dollars to a private firm to вҖңspyвҖқ on student protesters and academics, including Palestine supporters and animal rights activists.

рҹҮ§рҹҮ¬ Electoral shift: The center-left Progressive Bulgaria party appeared to win BulgariaвҖҷs election, according to exit polls. It beat the center-right GERB party, which has long dominated, but a coalition government will need to be formed.

рҹҮЁрҹҮ© Temporary truce: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and the M23 rebel group took first steps towards a long-term peace deal, agreeing to allow aid deliveries, release prisoners, and stop targeting civilians.

рҹҮІрҹҮҫ Devastating fire: A fire spread through a village in MalaysiaвҖҷs Sabah state, destroying roughly 1,000 homes and displacing thousands.


In 2018, as you can see in this video that re-emerged over the weekend and went viral online, Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer blamed Palestinians for there being вҖңno peace in the Middle EastвҖқ and invoked the Torah.

In 2026, Schumer continues to refuse to hold Israel accountable for war crimes and acts of ethnic cleansing, being one of seven Democrats to vote last week against blocking weapons to Israel. As Mehdi argued in FridayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ Senate Democrats need to do the right thing вҖ“ and replace him immediately.

Abdul El-SayedвҖҷs support is up 8 points in the latest Emerson poll, and he is now tied with Mallory McMorrow. I guess campaigning with Hasan Piker really hurt him, right? RIGHT??

Mike Waltz told ABCвҖҷs вҖҳThis WeekвҖҷ that вҖңall options are on the tableвҖқ in Iran, including destroying power plants and bridges, and rejected the idea that attacks on civilian infrastructure are war crimes.

The GOP is completely allergic to facts. When CNNвҖҷs Jake Tapper pressed Rep. Elise Stefanik on TrumpвҖҷs call to wipe out the вҖңwhole civilizationвҖқ of Iran, Stefanik refused to call the comments genocidal language. вҖңPresident Trump didnвҖҷt call for genocide, Jake. You are putting those words in his mouth,вҖқ she said.

The administration is seemingly escalating its efforts to claim the 2020 elections were rigged. FBI Director Kash Patel, a long-time election denier, told FoxвҖҷs Maria Bartiromo he has вҖңall the informationвҖқ he needs to make arrests soon.

Energy Secretary Chris Wright walked back claims that inflated gas prices would only last for вҖңweeks,вҖқ not вҖңmonths,вҖқ telling CNN he has no idea when prices will go below $3. вҖңThat could happen later this year,вҖқ Wright said. вҖңThat might happen until next year. But prices have likely peaked.вҖқ

The solution to gerrymandering is AI вҖ“ or so GOP Rep. Nicole Maliotakis proposed on CNNвҖҷs вҖҳState of the UnionвҖҷ as a way to produce fair districts, ahead of the Virginia redistricting referendum.

Monday, April 20: Elon Musk is set to be questioned by French cybercrime prosecutors in their ongoing investigation of X. Plus, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to address the House of Commons regarding his appointment of Epstein-affiliate Peter Mandelson as ambassador to the US. And the Supreme Court is scheduled to hear oral arguments in Sripetch v. SEC, which could strip the SEC of its regulatory powers.

Tuesday, April 21: Virginians are set to vote on a new electoral map in which Democrats could win up to 10 House seats. Plus, US judges will hear arguments over the Justice DepartmentвҖҷs efforts to force New Mexico to hand over voter data.

Wednesday, April 22: California gubernatorial candidates Chad Bianco, Katie Porter, Tom Steyer, and Steve Hilton will participate in a debate on NewsNation.

Saturday, April 25: Trump is set to headline вҖңthe most exclusive crypto & business conferenceвҖқ at Mar-a-Lago for the top 297 holders of his $TRUMP crypto memecoin. HeвҖҷs also expected to attend the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner for the first time as president.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.
